instructions: नीचे दिए गए Hindi sentences को English में translate करें। अपना answer लिखें, फिर 'Reveal Answer' button दबाएं।

questions.hindi: मैं एक छात्र हूँ।
questions.english: I am a student.
alternatives: I'm a student.
questions.hint: I + am + article + noun
questions.explanation: I के साथ हमेशा 'am' आता है। 'a student' में 'a' इसलिए क्योंकि student consonant (s) से शुरू होता है।

questions.hindi: वह एक शिक्षक है।
questions.english: He is a teacher.
alternatives: She is a teacher.
questions.hint: He/She + is + article + noun
questions.explanation: He/She/It के साथ 'is' आता है। Teacher consonant से शुरू होता है तो 'a teacher'।

questions.hindi: हम सब दोस्त हैं।
questions.english: We are all friends.
alternatives: We are friends.
questions.hint: We + are + noun
questions.explanation: We/You/They के साथ 'are' आता है।

questions.hindi: तुम बहुत होशियार हो।
questions.english: You are very smart.
alternatives: You are very intelligent.
You are very clever.
questions.hint: You + are + adjective
questions.explanation: 'very' एक adverb है जो adjective smart को describe करता है।

questions.hindi: यह किताब बहुत अच्छी है।
questions.english: This book is very good.
alternatives: This book is excellent.
This book is great.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective
questions.explanation: Demonstrative 'This' + noun + 'is' + adjective। यह basic sentence pattern है।

questions.hindi: मैं हर रोज़ स्कूल जाता हूँ।
questions.english: I go to school every day.
alternatives: I go to school daily.
questions.hint: I + base verb + object
questions.explanation: Simple Present में I के साथ base verb 'go' आता है। 'every day' time expression है।

questions.hindi: वह रोज़ सुबह दूध पीती है।
questions.english: She drinks milk every morning.
alternatives: She drinks milk daily in the morning.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + object + time
questions.explanation: She के साथ drink को drinks बनाते हैं (verb+s rule)। 'every morning' = time expression।

questions.hindi: हम office में काम करते हैं।
questions.english: We work in an office.
alternatives: We work at an office.
questions.hint: We + base verb + location
questions.explanation: 'office' vowel से शुरू होता है तो 'an office'। We के साथ base verb 'work'।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम चाय पीते हो?
questions.english: Do you drink tea?
alternatives: Do you have tea?
questions.hint: Do + you + base verb?
questions.explanation: Question बनाने के लिए Do + subject (you) + base verb। 'tea' uncountable noun है तो कोई article नहीं।

questions.hindi: क्या वह office जाता है?
questions.english: Does he go to office?
alternatives: Does he go to the office?
questions.hint: Does + he + base verb (no +s)?
questions.explanation: Does के साथ base verb (no +s)। 'Does he goes' WRONG — 'Does he go' CORRECT।

questions.hindi: मैं खाना नहीं खाता।
questions.english: I do not eat food.
alternatives: I don't eat food.
questions.hint: I + do not + base verb
questions.explanation: Negative में do not/don't + base verb। I don't eat = I do not eat।

questions.hindi: वह झूठ नहीं बोलती।
questions.english: She does not lie.
alternatives: She doesn't lie.
questions.hint: She + does not + base verb (no +s)
questions.explanation: She के साथ does not। lie verb 3rd person में 'lies' होगी, लेकिन does not के बाद base form 'lie' रहती है।

questions.hindi: यह क्या है?
questions.english: What is this?
alternatives: What's this?
questions.hint: What + is + this/that?
questions.explanation: WH question: What + is + demonstrative। Contraction: What's = What is।

questions.hindi: तुम कहाँ रहते हो?
questions.english: Where do you live?
alternatives: Where do you stay?
questions.hint: Where + do + you + base verb?
questions.explanation: Where + do + you + base verb। 'live' और 'stay' दोनों correct हैं।

questions.hindi: वह कब आता है?
questions.english: When does he come?
alternatives: When does he arrive?
questions.hint: When + does + he + base verb?
questions.explanation: When + does (he/she/it के लिए) + base verb (no +s)।

questions.hindi: तुम क्यों रोज़ देर से आते हो?
questions.english: Why do you always come late?
alternatives: Why are you always late?
questions.hint: Why + do + you + base verb?
questions.explanation: Why + do + you + base verb। 'always' frequency adverb verb से पहले आता है।

questions.hindi: वह अपना काम कैसे करता है?
questions.english: How does he do his work?
alternatives: How does he complete his work?
questions.hint: How + does + he + base verb + object?
questions.explanation: How + does + subject + base verb + object। 'his' possessive adjective है।

questions.hindi: कौन यहाँ रहता है?
questions.english: Who lives here?
alternatives: Who stays here?
questions.hint: Who + verb+s (Who = 3rd person singular)
questions.explanation: Who को 3rd person singular माना जाता है। इसलिए 'Who lives' (not 'Who live')।

questions.hindi: मैं हमेशा सच बोलता हूँ।
questions.english: I always tell the truth.
alternatives: I always speak the truth.
questions.hint: I + always + base verb + object
questions.explanation: Frequency adverb 'always' main verb से पहले आता है। 'tell the truth' = common expression।

questions.hindi: वह कभी-कभी बाहर खाना खाती है।
questions.english: She sometimes eats out.
alternatives: She sometimes eats outside.
She occasionally dines out.
questions.hint: She + sometimes + verb+s
questions.explanation: 'Sometimes' frequency adverb verb से पहले आता है। 'eats out' = phrasal verb, बाहर खाना।

questions.hindi: हम अक्सर cricket खेलते हैं।
questions.english: We often play cricket.
alternatives: We frequently play cricket.
questions.hint: We + often + base verb + object
questions.explanation: 'Often' = अक्सर। Verb से पहले आता है।

questions.hindi: मैं कभी झूठ नहीं बोलता।
questions.english: I never lie.
alternatives: I never tell lies.
questions.hint: I + never + base verb
questions.explanation: 'Never' already negative है, so no 'don't' needed। 'I never lie' = 'I don't ever lie'।

questions.hindi: वे लोग Delhi में रहते हैं।
questions.english: They live in Delhi.
alternatives: They stay in Delhi.
questions.hint: They + base verb + location
questions.explanation: They के साथ base verb। Location के पहले 'in' preposition।

questions.hindi: मेरी माँ खाना बनाती हैं।
questions.english: My mother cooks food.
alternatives: My mother makes food.
My mom cooks food.
questions.hint: My mother (3rd person) + verb+s
questions.explanation: 'My mother' = 3rd person singular, so cook → cooks।

questions.hindi: मेरे पिता बहुत मेहनती हैं।
questions.english: My father is very hardworking.
alternatives: My father is very diligent.
My dad is very hardworking.
questions.hint: My father + is + adjective
questions.explanation: 'My father' = 3rd person singular → 'is'। 'hardworking' adjective।

questions.hindi: मुझे English सीखना अच्छा लगता है।
questions.english: I like learning English.
alternatives: I enjoy learning English.
I love learning English.
questions.hint: I + like + verb+ing
questions.explanation: like/love/enjoy के बाद verb+ing आता है। 'learning' = learn+ing।

questions.hindi: वह संगीत सुनना पसंद करती है।
questions.english: She likes listening to music.
alternatives: She enjoys listening to music.
She loves listening to music.
questions.hint: She + likes + verb+ing + to + noun
questions.explanation: She के साथ 'likes' (verb+s)। listen + ing = listening। 'listen to music' = expression।

questions.hindi: हम football नहीं खेलते।
questions.english: We don't play football.
alternatives: We do not play football.
questions.hint: We + don't + base verb + object
questions.explanation: We के साथ negative में don't + base verb।

questions.hindi: क्या वह Hindi बोलती है?
questions.english: Does she speak Hindi?
alternatives: Does she know Hindi?
questions.hint: Does + she + base verb?
questions.explanation: 3rd person singular में question: Does + she + base verb।

questions.hindi: मैं सुबह 6 बजे उठता हूँ।
questions.english: I wake up at 6 o'clock in the morning.
alternatives: I get up at 6 AM.
I wake up at 6 AM.
questions.hint: I + verb + time
questions.explanation: Wake up = उठना (phrasal verb)। 'at 6 o'clock' = time expression। 'in the morning' = time of day।

questions.hindi: सूरज पूर्व में उगता है।
questions.english: The sun rises in the east.
alternatives: The sun comes up in the east.
questions.hint: The + noun + verb+s + location
questions.explanation: 'The sun' — specific noun, so 'the'। 'rises' = rise+s (3rd person)। 'in the east' = direction।

questions.hindi: बिल्लियाँ मछली खाती हैं।
questions.english: Cats eat fish.
alternatives: Cats like to eat fish.
questions.hint: Plural noun + base verb (no 's')
questions.explanation: Plural subjects (Cats) के साथ base verb (eat, no 's')।

questions.hindi: पानी 100 डिग्री पर उबलता है।
questions.english: Water boils at 100 degrees.
alternatives: Water boils at 100°C.
questions.hint: Water (uncountable, singular) + verb+s
questions.explanation: Water = uncountable noun, singular verb → boils। Scientific fact।

questions.hindi: मेरे पास एक laptop है।
questions.english: I have a laptop.
alternatives: I own a laptop.
questions.hint: I + have + article + noun
questions.explanation: Possession के लिए 'I have'। 'laptop' consonant से शुरू → 'a laptop'।

questions.hindi: उसके पास एक अच्छी नौकरी है।
questions.english: He has a good job.
alternatives: She has a good job.
questions.hint: He/She + has + article + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 3rd person के साथ 'has'। 'a good job' = article + adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: हमारे office में 50 लोग काम करते हैं।
questions.english: 50 people work in our office.
alternatives: There are 50 people in our office.
questions.hint: Number + plural noun + verb + location
questions.explanation: 50 people = plural subject → work (no 's')। 'our office' = possessive।

questions.hindi: वह हर शुक्रवार को gym जाता है।
questions.english: He goes to the gym every Friday.
alternatives: He visits the gym every Friday.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + to the + noun + every + day
questions.explanation: He → goes (go+es)। 'the gym' = specific place। 'every Friday' = frequency।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम्हारे पास phone है?
questions.english: Do you have a phone?
alternatives: Do you have a mobile?
Do you have a cell phone?
questions.hint: Do + you + have + article + noun?
questions.explanation: Question about possession: Do + you + have। 'a phone' = article + noun।

questions.hindi: मैं शाम को पढ़ता हूँ।
questions.english: I study in the evening.
alternatives: I study every evening.
questions.hint: I + base verb + time of day
questions.explanation: I → study (base verb)। 'in the evening' = time expression। 'in' + morning/afternoon/evening।

questions.hindi: वह बहुत तेज़ चलती है।
questions.english: She walks very fast.
alternatives: She walks very quickly.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + adverb
questions.explanation: She → walks (walk+s)। 'very fast' = adverb phrase। fast = manner adverb।

questions.hindi: मेरे दो भाई हैं।
questions.english: I have two brothers.
alternatives: I've got two brothers.
questions.hint: I + have + number + plural noun
questions.explanation: Family members: I have + number + plural noun (brothers)।

questions.hindi: हमारे school में एक बड़ा playground है।
questions.english: Our school has a big playground.
alternatives: There is a big playground in our school.
questions.hint: Our school + has + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'Our school' = 3rd person singular → has। 'a big playground' = article + adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: क्या वे लोग English बोलते हैं?
questions.english: Do they speak English?
alternatives: Do they know English?
questions.hint: Do + they + base verb + object?
questions.explanation: They → Do + they + base verb।

questions.hindi: मैं रात को देर से सोता हूँ।
questions.english: I sleep late at night.
alternatives: I go to bed late at night.
questions.hint: I + verb + adverb + time
questions.explanation: 'sleep late' = verb + manner adverb। 'at night' = time expression।
questions.grammarRule: I + [verb] + late/early + at night/in the morning

questions.hindi: उसका नाम Rahul है।
questions.english: His name is Rahul.
alternatives: His name's Rahul.
questions.hint: His/Her + noun + is + name
questions.explanation: 'His' = possessive adjective (male)। 'Her' = female। 'name is' = be verb।

questions.hindi: यह मेरा pen है।
questions.english: This is my pen.
alternatives: This pen is mine.
questions.hint: This + is + my + noun
questions.explanation: 'This is my pen' = demonstrative + be verb + possessive adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: वे लोग थके हुए हैं।
questions.english: They are tired.
alternatives: They look tired.
questions.hint: They + are + adjective
questions.explanation: 'They are tired' = subject + are + adjective (state)।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम busy हो?
questions.english: Are you busy?
alternatives: Are you occupied?
questions.hint: Are + you + adjective?
questions.explanation: Be verb question: Are + you + adjective?

questions.hindi: वह खुश नहीं है।
questions.english: She is not happy.
alternatives: She isn't happy.
She's not happy.
questions.hint: She + is not + adjective
questions.explanation: Negative be verb: is not / isn't। 'happy' = adjective।

questions.hindi: मैं doctor नहीं हूँ।
questions.english: I am not a doctor.
alternatives: I'm not a doctor.
questions.hint: I + am not + article + noun
questions.explanation: Negative: I am not / I'm not। 'a doctor' = article + noun (profession)।

questions.hindi: हमारी company बहुत अच्छी है।
questions.english: Our company is very good.
alternatives: Our company is excellent.
Our organization is very good.
questions.hint: Our + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: 'Our company' = possessive + noun। 'very good' = adverb + adjective।

questions.hindi: वह रोज़ morning walk पर जाता है।
questions.english: He goes for a morning walk every day.
alternatives: He goes for a walk every morning.
questions.hint: He + goes + for + article + noun + time
questions.explanation: 'go for a walk' = common expression। Goes (go+es, 3rd person)।
questions.grammarRule: He/She + goes for a [activity] + every day

questions.hindi: मुझे पानी चाहिए।
questions.english: I need water.
alternatives: I want water.
I would like some water.
questions.hint: I + need/want + object
questions.explanation: I need/want + noun। 'water' = uncountable noun, no article।

questions.hindi: क्या office में कोई है?
questions.english: Is anyone in the office?
alternatives: Is there anyone in the office?
Is somebody in the office?
questions.hint: Is + anyone + location?
questions.explanation: 'Is anyone in the office?' = question about presence।

questions.hindi: वे लोग हर साल Goa जाते हैं।
questions.english: They go to Goa every year.
alternatives: They visit Goa every year.
questions.hint: They + base verb + to + place + every year
questions.explanation: They → base verb (go)। 'every year' = annual frequency।

questions.hindi: यह exam बहुत कठिन है।
questions.english: This exam is very difficult.
alternatives: This exam is very hard.
This test is very tough.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: 'difficult/hard/tough' = synonyms। 'very' = intensifier।

questions.hindi: मैं English सीखना चाहता हूँ।
questions.english: I want to learn English.
alternatives: I wish to learn English.
questions.hint: I + want + to + base verb + object
questions.explanation: want + to + infinitive। 'want to learn' = verb + to + verb pattern।

questions.hindi: वह अपना घर साफ़ करती है।
questions.english: She cleans her house.
alternatives: She cleans her home.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: She → cleans (clean+s)। 'her house' = possessive adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम्हें coffee पसंद है?
questions.english: Do you like coffee?
alternatives: Do you enjoy coffee?
questions.hint: Do + you + like + noun
questions.explanation: Like का question: Do + you + like + noun।

questions.hindi: वह Google में काम करता है।
questions.english: He works at Google.
alternatives: He works for Google.
questions.hint: He + works + at/for + company
questions.explanation: 'work at' या 'work for' both correct for companies।

questions.hindi: मेरी बहन doctor है।
questions.english: My sister is a doctor.
alternatives: My sister is a physician.
questions.hint: My + relation + is + article + profession
questions.explanation: My sister = 3rd person singular → is। 'a doctor' = profession।

questions.hindi: बच्चे रोज़ खेलते हैं।
questions.english: Children play every day.
alternatives: Kids play daily.
questions.hint: Plural noun + base verb + frequency
questions.explanation: Children = plural → play (base verb, no 's')। 'every day' = frequency。

questions.hindi: क्या तुम gym जाते हो?
questions.english: Do you go to the gym?
alternatives: Do you work out?
questions.hint: Do + you + go + to the + place?
questions.explanation: 'the gym' = specific place (definite article)।
questions.grammarRule: Do + you + go to the + [specific place]?

questions.hindi: वह बहुत कम बोलता है।
questions.english: He speaks very little.
alternatives: He talks very little.
He rarely speaks.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + adverb
questions.explanation: 'very little' = manner/quantity adverb। He → speaks।

questions.hindi: हम सब एक team में काम करते हैं।
questions.english: We all work in a team.
alternatives: We work together as a team.
questions.hint: We all + base verb + location
questions.explanation: 'We all' = we + intensifier। 'in a team' = group context।

questions.hindi: मुझे नहीं पता।
questions.english: I don't know.
alternatives: I have no idea.
I am not sure.
questions.hint: I + don't + know
questions.explanation: 'I don't know' = I + do not + know। Most common expression।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम मुझे समझते हो?
questions.english: Do you understand me?
alternatives: Do you follow me?
questions.hint: Do + you + understand + object pronoun?
questions.explanation: Object pronoun: me (not I)। 'me' = I का object form।

questions.hindi: वे लोग office में कंप्यूटर से काम करते हैं।
questions.english: They work on computers in the office.
alternatives: They use computers at work.
questions.hint: They + work + on + noun + in + location
questions.explanation: 'work on computers' = verb + preposition + plural noun। 'in the office' = location।

questions.hindi: मैं तुमसे agree करता हूँ।
questions.english: I agree with you.
alternatives: I think you are right.
questions.hint: I + agree + with + object pronoun
questions.explanation: agree with = preposition + object pronoun। 'you' = object form (same as subject here)।

questions.hindi: क्या यह तुम्हारी किताब है?
questions.english: Is this your book?
alternatives: Is this book yours?
questions.hint: Is + this + your + noun?
questions.explanation: Be verb question: Is + demonstrative + possessive + noun?

questions.hindi: मैं इस बारे में कुछ नहीं जानता।
questions.english: I don't know anything about this.
alternatives: I have no knowledge about this.
questions.hint: I + don't + know + anything + about + this
questions.explanation: Negative with anything (not nothing — double negative avoid करें)।
questions.grammarRule: I + don't + know + anything + about + this/that

questions.hindi: वह अपने काम से खुश है।
questions.english: She is happy with her work.
alternatives: She is satisfied with her work.
questions.hint: She + is + adjective + with + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'happy with' = adjective + preposition। 'her work' = possessive adjective।

questions.hindi: हर कोई English बोलना चाहता है।
questions.english: Everyone wants to speak English.
alternatives: Everybody wants to learn English.
questions.hint: Everyone + verb+s (3rd person singular)
questions.explanation: 'Everyone/Everybody' = 3rd person singular → wants (not want)।

questions.hindi: मेरा favourite subject English है।
questions.english: My favourite subject is English.
alternatives: My favorite subject is English.
questions.hint: My + adjective + noun + is + subject name
questions.explanation: Favourite (British) = Favorite (American)। Both correct।

questions.hindi: वह दिन-रात मेहनत करता है।
questions.english: He works day and night.
alternatives: He works all day and night.
He works around the clock.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + time expression
questions.explanation: 'day and night' = idiom/expression = continuously। 'around the clock' = informal alternative।

questions.hindi: मैं आज बहुत थका हुआ हूँ।
questions.english: I am very tired today.
alternatives: I feel very tired today.
I am exhausted today.
questions.hint: I + am + adjective + time
questions.explanation: 'today' = time adverb, end में आता है। 'tired/exhausted' = synonyms।

questions.hindi: क्या Indian खाना तुम्हें पसंद है?
questions.english: Do you like Indian food?
alternatives: Do you enjoy Indian cuisine?
questions.hint: Do + you + like + adjective + noun?
questions.explanation: 'Indian' = adjective (nationality)। 'food/cuisine' = noun।

questions.hindi: वह बहुत अच्छे से English बोलती है।
questions.english: She speaks English very well.
alternatives: She is very fluent in English.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + object + adverb
questions.explanation: Adverb 'well' verb के बाद आता है object के बाद। 'very well' = intensifier + adverb।

questions.hindi: हम सुबह 9 बजे office पहुँचते हैं।
questions.english: We reach the office at 9 AM.
alternatives: We arrive at the office at 9 AM.
questions.hint: We + verb + the + office + at + time
questions.explanation: 'reach/arrive at' = location verbs। 'the office' = specific place। '9 AM' = time।

questions.hindi: मेरा phone number 9876543210 है।
questions.english: My phone number is 9876543210.
alternatives: My mobile number is 9876543210.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + number
questions.explanation: Personal information: My + [info type] + is + [value]।

questions.hindi: वह computer scientist है।
questions.english: He is a computer scientist.
alternatives: She is a computer scientist.
questions.hint: He/She + is + article + compound noun
questions.explanation: 'computer scientist' = compound noun। 'a' article because 'computer' starts with consonant।

questions.hindi: मैं अपने काम में बेहतर बनना चाहता हूँ।
questions.english: I want to improve in my work.
alternatives: I want to get better at my work.
I want to excel in my field.
questions.hint: I + want to + base verb + at/in + my + noun
questions.explanation: 'want to improve' = want + to + infinitive। 'improve in/at' = preposition के साथ।
questions.grammarRule: I + want to + [base verb] + in/at + my + [noun]

questions.hindi: क्या तुम मेरी help कर सकते हो?
questions.english: Can you help me?
alternatives: Could you help me?
Would you help me?
questions.hint: Can + you + base verb + object pronoun?
questions.explanation: 'Can you' = ability/request। 'me' = object pronoun।

questions.hindi: वे लोग 5 बजे office छोड़ते हैं।
questions.english: They leave the office at 5 o'clock.
alternatives: They finish work at 5 PM.
questions.hint: They + base verb + the + place + at + time
questions.explanation: They → leave (base verb)। 'the office' = specific। 'at 5 o'clock' = exact time।

questions.hindi: मुझे English में बोलना मुश्किल लगता है।
questions.english: I find speaking English difficult.
alternatives: I think speaking English is difficult.
Speaking English is difficult for me.
questions.hint: I + find + verb+ing + adjective
questions.explanation: 'find + verb+ing + adjective' = I find doing something difficult/easy।

questions.hindi: हमारे देश में बहुत सारी भाषाएँ बोली जाती हैं।
questions.english: Many languages are spoken in our country.
alternatives: Our country has many languages.
questions.hint: Many + plural noun + are + V3
questions.explanation: Passive voice (intro): Languages are spoken = passive।

questions.hindi: वह हर रात अपनी diary लिखती है।
questions.english: She writes her diary every night.
alternatives: She writes in her diary every night.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + possessive + noun + every night
questions.explanation: She → writes (write+s)。 'her diary' = possessive adjective。 'every night' = frequency。

questions.hindi: क्या यह सही है?
questions.english: Is this correct?
alternatives: Is this right?
Is this accurate?
questions.hint: Is + this + adjective?
questions.explanation: Short question: Is + demonstrative + adjective?

questions.hindi: मैं Delhi metro से travel करता हूँ।
questions.english: I travel by Delhi Metro.
alternatives: I commute by Delhi Metro.
I use the Delhi Metro.
questions.hint: I + verb + by + transport
questions.explanation: 'travel by' + transport mode। Metro = specific proper noun।

questions.hindi: वह lunch के बाद थोड़ा आराम करता है।
questions.english: He rests for a while after lunch.
alternatives: He takes a short break after lunch.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + for a while + after + noun
questions.explanation: 'for a while' = duration। 'after lunch' = time expression।

questions.hindi: मुझे music सुनना बहुत पसंद है।
questions.english: I really like listening to music.
alternatives: I absolutely love listening to music.
questions.hint: I + really + like + verb+ing + to + noun
questions.explanation: 'really' = intensifier। 'listening to music' = gerund phrase।

questions.hindi: क्या school बंद है?
questions.english: Is the school closed?
alternatives: Is the school shut?
questions.hint: Is + the + noun + adjective?
questions.explanation: 'the school' = specific। Is + the + noun + adjective।

questions.hindi: वे सब students बहुत होनहार हैं।
questions.english: Those students are very talented.
alternatives: All those students are very talented.
These students are very bright.
questions.hint: Those + plural noun + are + adjective
questions.explanation: 'Those students' = demonstrative + noun। Plural → are।

questions.hindi: मैं कल meeting में नहीं आ सकता।
questions.english: I cannot come to the meeting tomorrow.
alternatives: I can't attend the meeting tomorrow.
questions.hint: I + cannot/can't + base verb + article + noun + time
questions.explanation: cannot = can + not। 'the meeting' = specific meeting। 'tomorrow' = future time।

questions.hindi: वह बहुत ध्यान से सुनता है।
questions.english: He listens very carefully.
alternatives: He listens with great attention.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + manner adverb
questions.explanation: 'carefully' = manner adverb। He → listens (3rd person)。

questions.hindi: मेरे घर में 4 कमरे हैं।
questions.english: My house has 4 rooms.
alternatives: There are 4 rooms in my house.
questions.hint: My house + has + number + plural noun
questions.explanation: 'My house' = 3rd person → has। '4 rooms' = number + plural noun।

questions.hindi: वह Internet से पढ़ती है।
questions.english: She studies from the internet.
alternatives: She learns from the internet.
She uses the internet for studying.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + from + the + noun
questions.explanation: 'from the internet' = source preposition। 'the internet' = specific, definite article।

questions.hindi: यह movie 3 घंटे की है।
questions.english: This movie is 3 hours long.
alternatives: This film is 3 hours long.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + duration + long
questions.explanation: '3 hours long' = duration expression। 'This movie' = demonstrative + noun।

questions.hindi: क्या office में WiFi है?
questions.english: Is there WiFi in the office?
alternatives: Does the office have WiFi?
questions.hint: Is there + noun + in + location?
questions.explanation: Existence question: Is there + noun + in + location।

questions.hindi: मैं अपने सपने को पूरा करना चाहता हूँ।
questions.english: I want to fulfill my dream.
alternatives: I want to achieve my dream.
I want to accomplish my goals.
questions.hint: I + want + to + base verb + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'want to fulfill' = want + infinitive। 'my dream' = possessive + singular noun।

questions.hindi: वह सुबह जल्दी उठता है।
questions.english: He wakes up early in the morning.
alternatives: He gets up early every morning.
questions.hint: He + verb+s up + adverb + time
questions.explanation: 'wake up' = phrasal verb। 'early' = adverb। 3rd person → wakes।
questions.grammarRule: He + wakes up + early/late + in the morning

questions.hindi: क्या आपके पास visiting card है?
questions.english: Do you have a visiting card?
alternatives: Do you have a business card?
questions.hint: Do + you + have + article + compound noun?
questions.explanation: Formal: 'Do you have' (more polite than 'Have you got')।

questions.hindi: वह 2 साल से यहाँ काम कर रही है।
questions.english: She has been working here for 2 years.
alternatives: She has worked here for 2 years.
questions.hint: She + has been + verb+ing + for + duration
questions.explanation: Present Perfect Continuous: has been + verb+ing। 'for 2 years' = duration।

questions.hindi: मेरे boss बहुत strict हैं।
questions.english: My boss is very strict.
alternatives: My manager is very strict.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: 'My boss' = 3rd person singular → is। 'very strict' = intensifier + adjective।

questions.hindi: हम हर Monday को team meeting करते हैं।
questions.english: We have a team meeting every Monday.
alternatives: We hold a team meeting every Monday.
questions.hint: We + have + article + noun + every + day
questions.explanation: 'have a meeting' = expression। 'every Monday' = weekly frequency।

questions.hindi: वह बिना रुके काम करती है।
questions.english: She works without stopping.
alternatives: She works non-stop.
She works continuously.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + without + verb+ing
questions.explanation: 'without + verb+ing' = preposition + gerund।

questions.hindi: मैं एक नई किताब पढ़ रहा हूँ।
questions.english: I am reading a new book.
alternatives: I'm reading a new book.
questions.hint: I + am + verb+ing + article + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: am + verb+ing। 'a new book' = article + adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम भी इस बारे में जानते हो?
questions.english: Do you also know about this?
alternatives: Are you aware of this too?
questions.hint: Do + you + also + verb + about + this?
questions.explanation: 'also' = addition। 'know about' = verb + preposition।

questions.hindi: वे अगले हफ्ते office shift करेंगे।
questions.english: They will shift the office next week.
alternatives: They will relocate the office next week.
They are moving the office next week.
questions.hint: They + will + base verb + object + time
questions.explanation: Simple Future: will + base verb। 'next week' = future time expression।

questions.hindi: मुझे यह काम बहुत पसंद है।
questions.english: I like this work very much.
alternatives: I love this work.
I really enjoy this work.
questions.hint: I + like + this + noun + very much
questions.explanation: 'very much' = degree adverb, verb के बाद object के बाद।

questions.hindi: वह English में email लिखता है।
questions.english: He writes emails in English.
alternatives: He writes emails in the English language.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + plural noun + in + language
questions.explanation: He → writes (write+s)। 'emails' = plural। 'in English' = language preposition।

questions.hindi: यह meeting कब होगी?
questions.english: When will this meeting be?
alternatives: When is this meeting?
questions.hint: When + will + this + noun + be?
questions.explanation: Future question with WH word: When + will + subject + be?

questions.hindi: मैं अपने दोस्तों के साथ घूमने जाता हूँ।
questions.english: I go out with my friends.
alternatives: I hang out with my friends.
questions.hint: I + go out + with + possessive + plural noun
questions.explanation: 'go out' = phrasal verb। 'with my friends' = preposition + possessive + noun।

questions.hindi: वह अपनी family के लिए बहुत कुछ करता है।
questions.english: He does a lot for his family.
alternatives: He sacrifices a lot for his family.
questions.hint: He + does + a lot + for + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'does a lot' = expression। 'for his family' = purpose preposition।

questions.hindi: क्या यह product market में available है?
questions.english: Is this product available in the market?
alternatives: Is this product available in stores?
questions.hint: Is + this + noun + available + in + noun?
questions.explanation: 'available' = adjective। 'in the market' = location preposition।

questions.hindi: मैं अपने career में grow करना चाहता हूँ।
questions.english: I want to grow in my career.
alternatives: I want to advance in my career.
I aspire to grow professionally.
questions.hint: I + want to + base verb + in + my + noun
questions.explanation: 'want to grow' = want + infinitive। 'in my career' = context।
questions.grammarRule: I + want to + [grow/advance/excel] + in + my + career

questions.hindi: वह अपने phone पर बहुत time spend करता है।
questions.english: He spends a lot of time on his phone.
alternatives: He is always on his phone.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + a lot of + noun + on + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'spends time' = verb + noun collocation। 'on his phone' = preposition + possessive।

questions.hindi: क्या project पूरा हो गया?
questions.english: Is the project complete?
alternatives: Has the project been completed?
Is the project done?
questions.hint: Is + the + noun + adjective?
questions.explanation: Simple present question: Is + the + noun + adjective।

questions.hindi: वह English grammar बहुत अच्छे से जानता है।
questions.english: He knows English grammar very well.
alternatives: He has excellent knowledge of English grammar.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + noun + very well
questions.explanation: 'know very well' = verb + degree adverb। He → knows (3rd person)。

questions.hindi: हमें इस बारे में और जानकारी चाहिए।
questions.english: We need more information about this.
alternatives: We require more details about this.
questions.hint: We + need + more + uncountable noun + about + this
questions.explanation: 'information' = uncountable noun, no plural, no article 'a'। 'more information' = comparative quantity।
questions.grammarRule: We + need + more + [uncountable noun] + about + this

questions.hindi: मेरा जन्म 1998 में हुआ था।
questions.english: I was born in 1998.
alternatives: I was born in the year 1998.
questions.hint: I + was born + in + year
questions.explanation: Passive: 'was born' = be verb past + V3 (born)। Year से पहले 'in'।

questions.hindi: उसने कहा कि वह आएगा।
questions.english: He said that he will come.
alternatives: He said he would come.
questions.hint: He + said + that + he + will/would + base verb
questions.explanation: Reported speech: said + that + subject + will/would + verb।
questions.grammarRule: He/She + said + that + he/she + will + [base verb]

questions.hindi: क्या तुम कभी विदेश गए हो?
questions.english: Have you ever been abroad?
alternatives: Have you ever traveled abroad?
Have you ever been to a foreign country?
questions.hint: Have + you + ever + been + location?
questions.explanation: Present Perfect with 'ever' for life experience। 'been abroad' = visited foreign country।
questions.grammarRule: Have + you + ever + been to / done + [experience]?

questions.hindi: वह office में सबसे hard काम करता है।
questions.english: He works the hardest in the office.
alternatives: He is the most hardworking in the office.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + the + superlative + in + location
questions.explanation: Superlative adverb: hardest। 'works the hardest' = works most diligently।

questions.hindi: मुझे नहीं पता कि वह कहाँ है।
questions.english: I don't know where he is.
alternatives: I have no idea where he is.
questions.hint: I + don't know + WH + subject + be verb
questions.explanation: Indirect question: don't know + where + subject + is (statement order, not question order)।

questions.hindi: यह restaurant बहुत महँगा है।
questions.english: This restaurant is very expensive.
alternatives: This restaurant is quite pricey.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective
questions.explanation: 'expensive/pricey' = adjective। 'This restaurant' = demonstrative + noun।

questions.hindi: वह रोज़ 8 घंटे काम करती है।
questions.english: She works 8 hours a day.
alternatives: She works for 8 hours every day.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + number + hours + a day
questions.explanation: '8 hours a day' = duration expression। 'a day' = per day।

questions.hindi: मैं खुद से decision लेता हूँ।
questions.english: I make decisions on my own.
alternatives: I make decisions by myself.
I take decisions independently.
questions.hint: I + make + plural noun + on my own
questions.explanation: 'make decisions' = collocation। 'on my own' = by myself = independently।
questions.grammarRule: I + make + [decisions] + on my own / by myself

questions.hindi: उनका घर मेरे घर से बड़ा है।
questions.english: Their house is bigger than mine.
alternatives: Their house is larger than my house.
questions.hint: Their + noun + is + comparative + than + possessive pronoun
questions.explanation: Comparative: bigger (big+g+er)। 'than mine' = than + possessive pronoun।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम मुझे समझ पा रहे हो?
questions.english: Are you able to understand me?
alternatives: Can you understand me?
Do you follow me?
questions.hint: Are + you + able to + base verb + object?
questions.explanation: 'able to' = ability। object pronoun 'me'।

questions.hindi: वह अपनी life में बहुत आगे जाएगी।
questions.english: She will go far in her life.
alternatives: She will achieve a lot in life.
questions.hint: She + will + base verb + far + in + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'go far' = idiom = succeed greatly। Future: will + base verb।

questions.hindi: मेरे पास बहुत कम time है।
questions.english: I have very little time.
alternatives: I don't have much time.
questions.hint: I + have + very little + uncountable noun
questions.explanation: 'very little' with uncountable noun (time)। 'not much' also correct।
questions.grammarRule: I + have + very little/not much + [uncountable noun]

questions.hindi: वे लोग project को time पर complete करते हैं।
questions.english: They complete the project on time.
alternatives: They finish the project on time.
They deliver the project on time.
questions.hint: They + base verb + the + noun + on time
questions.explanation: 'on time' = punctual expression। 'complete/finish/deliver' = synonyms।

questions.hindi: मुझे किसी की ज़रूरत है।
questions.english: I need someone.
alternatives: I need somebody.
questions.hint: I + need + indefinite pronoun
questions.explanation: 'someone/somebody' = indefinite pronoun। Used in positive sentences।

questions.hindi: वह कभी late नहीं आता।
questions.english: He never comes late.
alternatives: He is never late.
questions.hint: He + never + verb+s (no 'don't')
questions.explanation: 'never' already negative। No 'doesn't' needed।

questions.hindi: मैं तुमसे बात करना चाहता हूँ।
questions.english: I want to talk to you.
alternatives: I would like to speak with you.
I need to talk to you.
questions.hint: I + want + to + base verb + to + you
questions.explanation: 'want to talk' = want + infinitive। 'talk to' = verb + preposition।
questions.grammarRule: I + want to + talk/speak + to/with + you

questions.hindi: क्या यह room खाली है?
questions.english: Is this room vacant?
alternatives: Is this room empty?
Is this room available?
questions.hint: Is + this + noun + adjective?
questions.explanation: 'vacant/empty/available' = synonyms for unoccupied।

questions.hindi: वह हमेशा positive रहती है।
questions.english: She always stays positive.
alternatives: She is always positive.
She always remains positive.
questions.hint: She + always + verb+s + adjective
questions.explanation: 'stays/remains positive' = linking verb + adjective।

questions.hindi: हम एक दूसरे की help करते हैं।
questions.english: We help each other.
alternatives: We assist each other.
We support each other.
questions.hint: We + base verb + each other
questions.explanation: 'each other' = reciprocal pronoun। Plural subject।

questions.hindi: यह idea बहुत innovative है।
questions.english: This idea is very innovative.
alternatives: This idea is very creative.
This concept is very original.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective
questions.explanation: 'innovative/creative/original' = related adjectives।

questions.hindi: वह किताब पढ़ने में घंटों लगाता है।
questions.english: He spends hours reading books.
alternatives: He spends hours in reading.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + plural noun + verb+ing + plural noun
questions.explanation: 'spends hours' = time collocation। 'reading' = gerund।

questions.hindi: मुझे कुछ कहना है।
questions.english: I have something to say.
alternatives: There is something I want to say.
questions.hint: I + have + something + to + base verb
questions.explanation: 'have something to say' = have + indefinite pronoun + infinitive。

questions.hindi: क्या सब ठीक है?
questions.english: Is everything all right?
alternatives: Is everything okay?
Is all well?
questions.hint: Is + everything + adjective phrase?
questions.explanation: 'everything' = indefinite pronoun (singular) → is। 'all right/okay' = adjective phrase।

questions.hindi: मैं अपने goal के बारे में serious हूँ।
questions.english: I am serious about my goal.
alternatives: I am committed to my goal.
questions.hint: I + am + adjective + about + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'serious about' = adjective + preposition। 'my goal' = possessive + noun।

questions.hindi: वह हर situation में calm रहता है।
questions.english: He stays calm in every situation.
alternatives: He remains calm in every situation.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + adjective + in + every + noun
questions.explanation: 'stays/remains calm' = linking verb + adjective। 'in every situation' = context।

questions.hindi: इस company में नौकरी मिलना मुश्किल है।
questions.english: It is difficult to get a job in this company.
alternatives: Getting a job in this company is difficult.
questions.hint: It + is + adjective + to + base verb + article + noun + in + this + noun
questions.explanation: 'It is + adjective + to + infinitive' = formal sentence pattern।

questions.hindi: वे लोग एक-दूसरे से बात नहीं करते।
questions.english: They don't talk to each other.
alternatives: They don't speak to each other.
questions.hint: They + don't + base verb + to + each other
questions.explanation: Negative reciprocal: don't + verb + to + each other।

questions.hindi: मुझे अभी जाना है।
questions.english: I have to go now.
alternatives: I need to leave now.
I must go now.
questions.hint: I + have to + base verb + time adverb
questions.explanation: 'have to' = obligation/necessity। 'now' = immediate time adverb।

questions.hindi: क्या office में smoking allowed है?
questions.english: Is smoking allowed in the office?
alternatives: Is smoking permitted in the office?
questions.hint: Is + verb+ing + adjective + in + article + noun?
questions.explanation: 'smoking' = gerund as subject। 'allowed/permitted' = adjective।

questions.hindi: वह एक मेहनती और ईमानदार इंसान है।
questions.english: He is a hardworking and honest person.
alternatives: He is a diligent and sincere individual.
questions.hint: He + is + article + adjective + and + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Multiple adjectives: article + adjective + and + adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: मैं project deadline से पहले काम complete कर दूँगा।
questions.english: I will complete the work before the project deadline.
alternatives: I will finish the work before the deadline.
questions.hint: I + will + base verb + article + noun + before + article + noun
questions.explanation: Simple Future: will + base verb। 'before the deadline' = time clause।

questions.hindi: यह report अभी ready नहीं है।
questions.english: This report is not ready yet.
alternatives: This report isn't ready yet.
questions.hint: This + noun + is not + adjective + yet
questions.explanation: 'yet' = negative context में = still not। 'This report' = demonstrative + noun।

questions.hindi: वह हिंदी और English दोनों बोल सकता है।
questions.english: He can speak both Hindi and English.
alternatives: He speaks both Hindi and English.
questions.hint: He + can + base verb + both + noun + and + noun
questions.explanation: Modal 'can' for ability. 'both...and' = correlative conjunction।

questions.hindi: मुझे इस job में कोई interest नहीं।
questions.english: I have no interest in this job.
alternatives: I am not interested in this job.
questions.hint: I + have + no + noun + in + this + noun
questions.explanation: 'have no interest' = absence of interest। Formal expression।

questions.hindi: उन्होंने मुझे एक नई opportunity दी।
questions.english: They gave me a new opportunity.
alternatives: They offered me a new opportunity.
questions.hint: They + past verb + me + article + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Past tense: gave (give→gave). Indirect object: me। Direct object: a new opportunity।

questions.hindi: वह presentation बहुत impressive थी।
questions.english: That presentation was very impressive.
alternatives: The presentation was very impressive.
questions.hint: That/The + noun + was + adjective
questions.explanation: Past be verb: was। 'impressive' = adjective।

questions.hindi: क्या आप मेरी बात सुन रहे हैं?
questions.english: Are you listening to me?
alternatives: Are you paying attention to me?
questions.hint: Are + you + verb+ing + to + object pronoun?
questions.explanation: Present Continuous question: Are + you + verb+ing। 'listening to' = verb + preposition।

questions.hindi: यह मेरी ज़िंदगी की सबसे बड़ी गलती थी।
questions.english: This was the biggest mistake of my life.
alternatives: That was the greatest mistake of my life.
questions.hint: This + was + the + superlative + noun + of + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: Superlative: biggest (big+g+est)। 'of my life' = of + possessive + noun।
questions.grammarRule: This + was + the + [superlative] + [noun] + of + my + life

questions.hindi: वह हर किसी से अच्छे से पेश आती है।
questions.english: She treats everyone well.
alternatives: She behaves well with everyone.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + everyone + adverb
questions.explanation: She → treats (treat+s)। 'everyone' = indefinite pronoun (singular)। 'well' = manner adverb।

questions.hindi: मैं रोज़ नए English words सीखता हूँ।
questions.english: I learn new English words every day.
alternatives: I study new English vocabulary every day.
questions.hint: I + base verb + adjective + plural noun + every day
questions.explanation: I → learn (base verb)। 'new English words' = adjective + noun chain।

questions.hindi: हम सब मिलकर एक अच्छी team बनाते हैं।
questions.english: We all make a great team together.
alternatives: Together, we make an excellent team.
questions.hint: We all + base verb + article + adjective + noun + together
questions.explanation: 'make a team' = expression। 'together' = adverb of manner।

questions.hindi: मेरे पास अभी time नहीं है।
questions.english: I don't have time right now.
alternatives: I have no time right now.
questions.hint: I + don't + have + noun + right now
questions.explanation: 'right now' = emphatic time expression। 'don't have time' = no time।

questions.hindi: वह अपनी problems को अकेले solve करता है।
questions.english: He solves his problems alone.
alternatives: He handles his problems by himself.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + possessive + plural noun + adverb
questions.explanation: 'alone/by himself' = independence adverb। He → solves (3rd person)।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम कल office आओगे?
questions.english: Will you come to the office tomorrow?
alternatives: Are you coming to the office tomorrow?
questions.hint: Will + you + base verb + to + the + noun + time?
questions.explanation: Future: Will + you + base verb। 'tomorrow' = future time।
questions.grammarRule: Will + you + [base verb] + to + the + [place] + tomorrow?

questions.hindi: वह सबको motivate करती है।
questions.english: She motivates everyone.
alternatives: She inspires everyone.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + everyone
questions.explanation: She → motivates (motivate+s)। 'everyone' = indefinite pronoun।

questions.hindi: मैं इस बारे में agree नहीं करता।
questions.english: I don't agree with this.
alternatives: I disagree with this.
I don't see it that way.
questions.hint: I + don't + agree + with + this
questions.explanation: 'agree with' = verb + preposition। Negative: don't agree।

questions.hindi: वे लोग Monday से Friday तक office जाते हैं।
questions.english: They go to the office from Monday to Friday.
alternatives: They work Monday through Friday.
questions.hint: They + base verb + from + day + to + day
questions.explanation: 'from...to...' = range expression। Monday to Friday = 5 days।

questions.hindi: यह बहुत ज़रूरी काम है।
questions.english: This is very important work.
alternatives: This is crucial work.
This is significant work.
questions.hint: This + is + adverb + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'This is' + very + adjective + noun। 'important' = adjective।

questions.hindi: मैं अपने dream को कभी नहीं छोड़ूँगा।
questions.english: I will never give up on my dream.
alternatives: I will never abandon my dream.
I will never stop pursuing my dream.
questions.hint: I + will + never + phrasal verb + on + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'give up on' = phrasal verb = छोड़ देना। 'will never' = future negative।
questions.grammarRule: I + will + never + give up on + my + [dream/goal]

questions.hindi: वह बीमार है, इसलिए office नहीं आई।
questions.english: She is sick, so she did not come to the office.
alternatives: She is ill, so she didn't come to work.
questions.hint: She + is + adjective + so + she + did not + base verb
questions.explanation: 'so' = cause-effect conjunction। Past: 'did not come'।
questions.grammarRule: She + is + [adjective], + so + she + didn't + [base verb]

questions.hindi: मैं English सीखने में बहुत time invest करता हूँ।
questions.english: I invest a lot of time in learning English.
alternatives: I spend a lot of time learning English.
questions.hint: I + verb + a lot of + noun + in + verb+ing + noun
questions.explanation: 'invest time in' = formal expression। 'learning' = gerund।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम अपने काम से खुश हो?
questions.english: Are you happy with your work?
alternatives: Are you satisfied with your job?
questions.hint: Are + you + adjective + with + possessive + noun?
questions.explanation: 'happy with' = adjective + preposition। 'your work' = possessive。

questions.hindi: वह एक अच्छे family से है।
questions.english: He is from a good family.
alternatives: He comes from a good family.
questions.hint: He + is + from + article + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'is from' = origin। 'a good family' = article + adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: मेरा English बोलना अभी भी improve हो रहा है।
questions.english: My English is still improving.
alternatives: I am still improving my English.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + still + verb+ing
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: is + verb+ing। 'still' = continuing action।

questions.hindi: वह नई technology को जल्दी सीख लेता है।
questions.english: He learns new technology quickly.
alternatives: He picks up new technology fast.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + adjective + noun + adverb
questions.explanation: He → learns (3rd person)। 'quickly' = manner adverb, verb के बाद।

questions.hindi: मैं अपने आप पर विश्वास रखता हूँ।
questions.english: I believe in myself.
alternatives: I have confidence in myself.
I trust myself.
questions.hint: I + believe + in + reflexive pronoun
questions.explanation: 'believe in' = verb + preposition। 'myself' = reflexive pronoun।

questions.hindi: क्या आज weather ठीक है?
questions.english: Is the weather good today?
alternatives: Is it nice weather today?
How is the weather today?
questions.hint: Is + the + noun + adjective + time?
questions.explanation: Weather question: Is + the + weather + adjective। 'today' = time adverb।

questions.hindi: वह जो भी करती है उसमें best देती है।
questions.english: She gives her best in everything she does.
alternatives: She puts her best into everything she does.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + possessive + noun + in + everything
questions.explanation: 'gives her best' = expression। 'everything she does' = relative clause।
questions.grammarRule: He/She + gives + her/his + best + in + everything

questions.hindi: मुझे अपनी mistake पता चल गई।
questions.english: I realized my mistake.
alternatives: I came to know my mistake.
I became aware of my mistake.
questions.hint: I + past verb + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: Realized = realize का past। 'my mistake' = possessive + noun।

questions.hindi: वे लोग हमेशा support करते हैं।
questions.english: They always support us.
alternatives: They always help us.
They always stand by us.
questions.hint: They + always + base verb + object pronoun
questions.explanation: They → always + base verb (no 's')। 'us' = object pronoun (we का object form)।

questions.hindi: मैं Mumbai में पैदा हुआ था।
questions.english: I was born in Mumbai.
alternatives: I'm from Mumbai originally.
questions.hint: I + was born + in + city
questions.explanation: 'was born' = passive (I + be verb past + V3)। 'in Mumbai' = place of birth।

questions.hindi: वह अपनी team को lead करता है।
questions.english: He leads his team.
alternatives: He manages his team.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: He → leads (lead+s)。 'his team' = possessive adjective + noun。

questions.hindi: मैं अभी call पर हूँ।
questions.english: I am on a call right now.
alternatives: I'm on the phone right now.
questions.hint: I + am + on + article + noun + time adverb
questions.explanation: 'on a call' = common office expression। 'right now' = emphatic present।
questions.grammarRule: I + am + on + a + call/meeting + right now

questions.hindi: क्या यह meeting important है?
questions.english: Is this meeting important?
alternatives: Is this meeting significant?
questions.hint: Is + this + noun + adjective?
questions.explanation: Simple question with demonstrative + be verb + adjective।

questions.hindi: वह हर साल एक नई skill सीखती है।
questions.english: She learns a new skill every year.
alternatives: She acquires a new skill every year.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + article + adjective + noun + every year
questions.explanation: She → learns. 'a new skill' = article + adjective + noun. 'every year' = annual.

questions.hindi: मुझे English grammar rules याद हैं।
questions.english: I remember the English grammar rules.
alternatives: I know the English grammar rules.
questions.hint: I + verb + the + noun + noun
questions.explanation: 'the English grammar rules' = specific rules (definite article)। I → remember।

questions.hindi: वह meetings में बहुत ध्यान से notes लेता है।
questions.english: He takes notes very carefully in meetings.
alternatives: He note-takes very carefully during meetings.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + plural noun + adverb + in + plural noun
questions.explanation: 'takes notes' = expression। 'very carefully' = degree + manner adverb।
questions.grammarRule: He/She + takes notes + very carefully + in + meetings

questions.hindi: इस course को complete करने के बाद मेरी life बदल जाएगी।
questions.english: After completing this course, my life will change.
alternatives: My life will change after I complete this course.
questions.hint: After + verb+ing + noun, + possessive + noun + will + base verb
questions.explanation: 'After completing' = gerund phrase। Future: will change।

questions.hindi: वह customer की बात बहुत ध्यान से सुनती है।
questions.english: She listens to the customer very carefully.
alternatives: She pays close attention to the customer.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + to + the + noun + adverb
questions.explanation: She → listens (listen+s). 'listen to' = verb + preposition. 'the customer' = specific.
questions.grammarRule: She + listens to + the + [person] + very carefully

questions.hindi: मैं हर रात 10 नए words याद करता हूँ।
questions.english: I memorize 10 new words every night.
alternatives: I learn 10 new words every night.
questions.hint: I + base verb + number + adjective + plural noun + every night
questions.explanation: I → memorize (base verb). '10 new words' = number + adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: वह एक बहुत अच्छा listener है।
questions.english: He is a very good listener.
alternatives: He is an excellent listener.
questions.hint: He + is + article + adverb + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'a very good listener' = article + intensifier + adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: क्या आप interview के लिए तैयार हैं?
questions.english: Are you ready for the interview?
alternatives: Have you prepared for the interview?
questions.hint: Are + you + adjective + for + the + noun?
questions.explanation: 'ready for' = adjective + preposition. 'the interview' = specific event.

questions.hindi: वे लोग अपने काम को लेकर serious रहते हैं।
questions.english: They are serious about their work.
alternatives: They take their work seriously.
questions.hint: They + are + adjective + about + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'serious about' = adjective + preposition. 'their work' = possessive.

questions.hindi: मुझे यह opportunity मिलनी चाहिए।
questions.english: I should get this opportunity.
alternatives: I deserve this opportunity.
I ought to get this opportunity.
questions.hint: I + should + base verb + this + noun
questions.explanation: 'should' = modal for advice/expectation. 'this opportunity' = demonstrative + noun.

questions.hindi: वह अपने colleagues के साथ बहुत अच्छे से रहती है।
questions.english: She gets along well with her colleagues.
alternatives: She has a good relationship with her colleagues.
questions.hint: She + phrasal verb + adverb + with + possessive + plural noun
questions.explanation: 'gets along with' = phrasal verb = अच्छे से रहना. 'well' = manner adverb.

questions.hindi: मैं daily basis पर English practice करता हूँ।
questions.english: I practice English on a daily basis.
alternatives: I practice English every day.
I practice English daily.
questions.hint: I + base verb + object + on + article + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'on a daily basis' = formal expression for 'every day'.

questions.hindi: क्या तुम्हें यह समझ आया?
questions.english: Did you understand this?
alternatives: Have you understood this?
Did you get this?
questions.hint: Did + you + base verb + this?
questions.explanation: Past tense question: Did + you + base verb (not understood).

questions.hindi: वह कड़ी मेहनत से सफल हुई।
questions.english: She succeeded through hard work.
alternatives: She achieved success through hard work.
questions.hint: She + past verb + through + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'succeeded' = succeed का V2. 'through hard work' = means/manner preposition.

questions.hindi: मेरी English हर रोज़ बेहतर हो रही है।
questions.english: My English is getting better every day.
alternatives: My English is improving every day.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + getting + comparative + every day
questions.explanation: 'getting + adjective' = change expression. 'better' = comparative of 'good'.
questions.grammarRule: My + [skill] + is + getting + better/stronger + every day

questions.hindi: Day 1 पूरा हो गया! मैंने बहुत कुछ सीखा।
questions.english: Day 1 is complete! I have learned a lot.
alternatives: Day 1 is done! I have learned so much.
questions.hint: Subject + is + adjective! I + have + past verb + quantity
questions.explanation: 'is complete' = be verb + adjective. Present Perfect: have + learned (V3).
questions.grammarRule: I + have + learned + a lot + today

questions.hindi: मैं खाना नहीं बनाता।
questions.english: I do not cook food.
alternatives: I don't cook food.
I don't cook.
questions.hint: I + do not + base verb
questions.explanation: Negative simple present with do not/don't.

questions.hindi: वह office नहीं जाता।
questions.english: He does not go to office.
alternatives: He doesn't go to office.
He doesn't go to the office.
questions.hint: He + does not + base verb (no +s)
questions.explanation: 3rd person negative: does not + base verb.

questions.hindi: हम यहाँ नहीं रहते।
questions.english: We do not live here.
alternatives: We don't live here.
We don't stay here.
questions.hint: We + do not + base verb + location
questions.explanation: We → do not + base verb.

questions.hindi: मुझे coffee पसंद नहीं।
questions.english: I do not like coffee.
alternatives: I don't like coffee.
I'm not a fan of coffee.
questions.hint: I + do not + like + noun
questions.explanation: Preference negative: I don't like + noun.

questions.hindi: वह TV नहीं देखती।
questions.english: She does not watch TV.
alternatives: She doesn't watch TV.
She doesn't watch television.
questions.hint: She + does not + base verb + noun
questions.explanation: She → does not + watch (base form, no 's').

questions.hindi: तुम gym क्यों नहीं जाते?
questions.english: Why don't you go to the gym?
alternatives: Why do you not go to the gym?
questions.hint: Why + don't + you + base verb?
questions.explanation: WH question + negative: Why + don't + you + base verb?

questions.hindi: वह अखबार नहीं पढ़ता।
questions.english: He does not read the newspaper.
alternatives: He doesn't read newspapers.
He doesn't read the paper.
questions.hint: He + does not + read + the + noun
questions.explanation: He → does not + read. 'the newspaper' = specific/definite.

questions.hindi: मैं English movies नहीं देखता।
questions.english: I do not watch English movies.
alternatives: I don't watch English films.
questions.hint: I + do not + watch + adjective + plural noun
questions.explanation: Negative preference: I don't + watch + adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: वे लोग meat नहीं खाते।
questions.english: They do not eat meat.
alternatives: They don't eat meat.
They are vegetarian.
questions.hint: They + do not + eat + uncountable noun
questions.explanation: They → do not + eat. 'meat' = uncountable, no article.

questions.hindi: मुझे cricket में interest नहीं।
questions.english: I am not interested in cricket.
alternatives: I don't find cricket interesting.
Cricket doesn't interest me.
questions.hint: I + am not + adjective + in + noun
questions.explanation: 'interested in' = adjective + preposition. Negative: am not.

questions.hindi: वह ऑफिस में phone use नहीं करता।
questions.english: He does not use his phone in the office.
alternatives: He doesn't use his phone at work.
questions.hint: He + does not + use + possessive + noun + in + article + noun
questions.explanation: He → does not + use. 'his phone' = possessive. 'in the office' = location.
questions.grammarRule: He + doesn't + use + his + phone + in + the office

questions.hindi: यह idea practical नहीं है।
questions.english: This idea is not practical.
alternatives: This idea isn't practical.
This idea is impractical.
questions.hint: This + noun + is not + adjective
questions.explanation: Negative be verb: is not/isn't. 'practical' = adjective.

questions.hindi: मैं सोशल media पर ज़्यादा time नहीं बिताता।
questions.english: I do not spend much time on social media.
alternatives: I don't spend a lot of time on social media.
questions.hint: I + do not + spend + much + noun + on + noun
questions.explanation: 'much' with uncountable noun 'time'. 'on social media' = preposition.
questions.grammarRule: I + don't + spend + much/a lot of + time + on + [noun]

questions.hindi: वह हमेशा truth नहीं बोलती।
questions.english: She does not always tell the truth.
alternatives: She doesn't always tell the truth.
questions.hint: She + does not + always + base verb + the + noun
questions.explanation: Frequency adverb with negative: does not + always + base verb.

questions.hindi: हम weekend पर office नहीं जाते।
questions.english: We do not go to the office on weekends.
alternatives: We don't work on weekends.
We don't go to office on Saturdays and Sundays.
questions.hint: We + do not + go + to + noun + on + weekends
questions.explanation: We → do not + go. 'on weekends' = recurring time expression.
questions.grammarRule: We + don't + go to + the office + on weekends

questions.hindi: क्या तुम यहाँ रहते हो?
questions.english: Do you live here?
alternatives: Do you stay here?
Do you reside here?
questions.hint: Do + you + live + location?
questions.explanation: Simple present yes-no question: Do + you + base verb.

questions.hindi: क्या वे लोग English बोलते हैं?
questions.english: Do they speak English?
alternatives: Do they know English?
questions.hint: Do + they + base verb + object?
questions.explanation: They → Do + they + base verb. No does for 'they'.

questions.hindi: क्या आप India से हैं?
questions.english: Are you from India?
alternatives: Are you Indian?
questions.hint: Are + you + from + country?
questions.explanation: Be verb question about origin: Are + you + from + country.

questions.hindi: क्या वह doctor है?
questions.english: Is he a doctor?
alternatives: Is she a doctor?
Is he/she a physician?
questions.hint: Is + he/she + article + profession?
questions.explanation: Be verb question: Is + pronoun + a/an + profession.

questions.hindi: क्या तुम्हें यह film पसंद आई?
questions.english: Did you like this film?
alternatives: Did you enjoy this movie?
questions.hint: Did + you + base verb + this + noun?
questions.explanation: Past question: Did + you + base verb (not liked). 'this film' = demonstrative.

questions.hindi: क्या कल बारिश हुई?
questions.english: Did it rain yesterday?
alternatives: Was there rain yesterday?
questions.hint: Did + it + base verb + time?
questions.explanation: Past question: Did + it + rain (base form). 'yesterday' = past time.

questions.hindi: क्या meeting अच्छी रही?
questions.english: Did the meeting go well?
alternatives: Was the meeting good?
How did the meeting go?
questions.hint: Did + the + noun + go + adverb?
questions.explanation: Past question: Did + the + noun + go + well. 'go well' = expression.

questions.hindi: क्या तुमने उसे call किया?
questions.english: Did you call him?
alternatives: Did you phone him?
Did you ring him?
questions.hint: Did + you + base verb + object pronoun?
questions.explanation: Past question: Did + you + call (base form). 'him' = object pronoun.

questions.hindi: क्या आज office खुला है?
questions.english: Is the office open today?
alternatives: Is the office open on this day?
questions.hint: Is + the + noun + adjective + time?
questions.explanation: Present question: Is + the + noun + open/closed. 'today' = time.

questions.hindi: क्या तुम्हें salary मिल गई?
questions.english: Did you receive your salary?
alternatives: Have you got your salary?
Did you get your salary?
questions.hint: Did + you + receive + possessive + noun?
questions.explanation: Past question: Did + you + receive/get. 'your salary' = possessive.

questions.hindi: वह आज काम पर कब आएगा?
questions.english: When will he come to work today?
alternatives: What time will he come to office?
questions.hint: When + will + he + base verb + to + noun + time?
questions.explanation: WH future question: When + will + he + base verb.

questions.hindi: तुम इतनी देर से क्यों आए?
questions.english: Why did you come so late?
alternatives: Why were you late?
questions.hint: Why + did + you + base verb + adverb?
questions.explanation: Past WH question: Why + did + you + come. 'so late' = degree + adverb.
questions.grammarRule: Why + did + you + come/arrive + so late?

questions.hindi: इस project में कितना time लगेगा?
questions.english: How much time will this project take?
alternatives: How long will this project take?
questions.hint: How much + noun + will + subject + take?
questions.explanation: WH future question about time/duration: How much time will + subject + take.
questions.grammarRule: How much time + will + this + [noun] + take?

questions.hindi: तुम कहाँ पले-बढ़े हो?
questions.english: Where did you grow up?
alternatives: Where were you raised?
Where are you from originally?
questions.hint: Where + did + you + phrasal verb?
questions.explanation: 'grow up' = phrasal verb = पलना-बढ़ना. Past: did + grow (base form).

questions.hindi: तुमने यह काम कैसे किया?

questions.hindi: मैं अपने आप को express करना सीख रहा हूँ।

alternatives: My English wasn't very good.
