questions.explanation: Past: was not/wasn't. 'very good' = intensifier + adjective.

questions.hindi: लेकिन अब मैं बेहतर हो रहा हूँ।
questions.english: But now I am getting better.
alternatives: But now I am improving.
questions.hint: But + now + I + am + verb+ing + comparative adjective
questions.explanation: 'getting better' = become + comparative. 'But now' = contrast + time.
questions.grammarRule: But now + I + am + getting + better/stronger

questions.hindi: वे सब मिलकर एक अच्छा काम करते हैं।
questions.english: They all do a good job together.
alternatives: They all work well together.
questions.hint: They all + base verb + article + adjective + noun + adverb
questions.explanation: They all → do (base). 'a good job' = expression. 'together' = manner adverb.
questions.grammarRule: They + all + do + a + good + job + together

questions.hindi: वह घबराए बिना public में बोलती है।
questions.english: She speaks in public without getting nervous.
alternatives: She speaks publicly without nervousness.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + in + noun + without + verb+ing + adjective
questions.explanation: She → speaks. 'without getting nervous' = without + gerund phrase.
questions.grammarRule: She + speaks + in public + without + getting + nervous

questions.hindi: मैं Day 1 से Day 75 तक हर दिन practice करूँगा।
questions.english: I will practice every day from Day 1 to Day 75.
alternatives: I will study every single day from Day 1 to Day 75.
questions.hint: I + will + base verb + every day + from + noun + to + noun
questions.explanation: Future: will + practice. 'from Day 1 to Day 75' = range expression.
questions.grammarRule: I + will + [practice/study] + every day + from + [start] + to + [end]

questions.hindi: वह बहुत fast English बोलती है।
questions.english: She speaks English very fast.
alternatives: She speaks English very quickly.
She speaks English very rapidly.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + language + adverb phrase
questions.explanation: She → speaks. 'very fast/quickly/rapidly' = manner adverb.

questions.hindi: मुझे यहाँ आकर बहुत खुशी हुई।
questions.english: I am very happy to be here.
alternatives: I am so glad to be here.
I feel very happy to be here.
questions.hint: I + am + adjective + to + be + location
questions.explanation: 'happy to be' = adjective + infinitive of be. Common formal expression.
questions.grammarRule: I + am + very + happy/glad + to + be + here

questions.hindi: उसका interview कल 10 बजे है।
questions.english: His interview is at 10 o'clock tomorrow.
alternatives: Her interview is tomorrow at 10 AM.
questions.hint: His/Her + noun + is + at + time + time adverb
questions.explanation: 'His interview' = possessive + noun. 'at 10 o'clock' = specific time. 'tomorrow' = day.

questions.hindi: वह किसी भी topic पर बहुत अच्छे से बोल सकती है।
questions.english: She can speak very well on any topic.
alternatives: She is able to speak fluently on any topic.
questions.hint: She + can + base verb + manner + on + any + noun
questions.explanation: Modal 'can' for ability. She + can + speak. 'on any topic' = preposition + any + noun.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + can + speak + very well + on + any + topic

questions.hindi: हम सब अपने-अपने goals की तरफ काम कर रहे हैं।
questions.english: We are all working towards our own goals.
alternatives: All of us are working towards our individual goals.
questions.hint: We + are + all + verb+ing + towards + possessive + adjective + plural noun
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: are + working. 'towards' = direction preposition. 'our own' = possessive + intensifier.
questions.grammarRule: We + are + all + working + towards + our + own + goals

questions.hindi: मेरे mentor ने मुझे English सीखने की advice दी।
questions.english: My mentor gave me advice to learn English.
alternatives: My mentor advised me to learn English.
questions.hint: My + noun + past verb + me + noun + to + base verb + noun
questions.explanation: Give → gave (irregular). 'advice to learn' = noun + infinitive. Indirect object: me.
questions.grammarRule: My + mentor + gave + me + advice + to + learn + English

questions.hindi: वह project अचानक बंद हो गया।
questions.english: That project was suddenly shut down.
alternatives: That project suddenly closed.
That project was abruptly terminated.
questions.hint: That + noun + was + adverb + phrasal verb (V3)
questions.explanation: Passive: was + shut down. 'suddenly' = adverb.
questions.grammarRule: That + project + was + suddenly + shut down/cancelled

questions.hindi: मैंने 75 दिनों में English fluent सीखने का decision लिया।
questions.english: I made the decision to learn English fluently in 75 days.
alternatives: I decided to learn English fluently in 75 days.
questions.hint: I + past verb + the + noun + to + base verb + noun + adverb + in + duration
questions.explanation: Make → made (irregular). 'the decision to' = noun + infinitive. 'in 75 days' = duration.
questions.grammarRule: I + made + the decision + to + [learn/achieve] + [goal] + in + [duration]

questions.hindi: वह office में सबसे पहले आती है।
questions.english: She comes to the office first.
alternatives: She is the first one to arrive at the office.
She arrives at the office earliest.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + to + article + noun + adverb
questions.explanation: She → comes. 'to the office' = direction. 'first' = sequence adverb.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + comes + to + the + office + first/earliest

questions.hindi: मुझे confidence की बहुत ज़रूरत है।
questions.english: I need confidence very much.
alternatives: I really need confidence.
I need a lot of confidence.
questions.hint: I + need + noun + adverb phrase
questions.explanation: I → need (base verb). 'very much' = degree adverb. 'confidence' = uncountable noun.

questions.hindi: वह English speaking में बहुत improvement कर रही है।
questions.english: She is making a lot of improvement in English speaking.
alternatives: She is improving a lot in English speaking.
questions.hint: She + is + verb+ing + article + a lot of + noun + in + noun + noun
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: is + making. 'a lot of improvement' = quantity expression.
questions.grammarRule: She + is + making + a lot of + improvement + in + [skill]

questions.hindi: मैं उन सभी लोगों का शुक्रगुज़ार हूँ जिन्होंने मेरी मदद की।
questions.english: I am grateful to all the people who helped me.
alternatives: I am thankful to everyone who helped me.
questions.hint: I + am + adjective + to + all + the + people + who + past verb + me
questions.explanation: 'grateful to' = adjective + preposition. 'who helped me' = relative clause.
questions.grammarRule: I + am + grateful/thankful + to + all + who + [helped/supported] + me

questions.hindi: वे लोग बहुत innovative thinking रखते हैं।
questions.english: They have very innovative thinking.
alternatives: They think in a very innovative way.
questions.hint: They + have + adverb + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: They → have (base verb for possession). 'innovative thinking' = adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: वह एक बहुत structured तरीके से काम करता है।
questions.english: He works in a very structured way.
alternatives: He works in an organized manner.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + in + article + adverb + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: He → works. 'in a very structured way' = manner preposition phrase.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + works + in + a + very + [structured/organized] + way

questions.hindi: मुझे पता था कि यह होगा।
questions.english: I knew this would happen.
alternatives: I was sure this would happen.
questions.hint: I + past verb + this + would + base verb
questions.explanation: Knew = know का past. 'this would happen' = future in the past.

questions.hindi: वह हर काम को दिल से करती है।
questions.english: She does every task with all her heart.
alternatives: She puts her heart into everything she does.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + every + noun + with + all + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: She → does. 'with all her heart' = prepositional phrase expressing dedication.
questions.grammarRule: She + does + every task + with + all + her + heart

questions.hindi: मुझे कुछ और practice की ज़रूरत है।
questions.english: I need some more practice.
alternatives: I require a bit more practice.
questions.hint: I + need + some + more + uncountable noun
questions.explanation: 'practice' = uncountable noun. 'some more' = some + comparative.

questions.hindi: वह बिना किसी preparation के presentation देता है।
questions.english: He gives presentations without any preparation.
alternatives: He presents without any preparation.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + plural noun + without + any + noun
questions.explanation: He → gives (3rd person). 'without any preparation' = preposition + determiner + noun.

questions.hindi: मेरे पास English improve करने के लिए बहुत resources हैं।
questions.english: I have many resources to improve my English.
alternatives: I have a lot of resources to improve my English.
questions.hint: I + have + many + plural noun + to + base verb + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'many resources' = quantifier + plural noun. 'to improve' = infinitive of purpose.
questions.grammarRule: I + have + many + resources + to + improve + my + English

questions.hindi: आज से मेरा नया chapter शुरू होता है।
questions.english: A new chapter begins for me from today.
alternatives: My new chapter starts from today.
questions.hint: Article + adjective + noun + verb+s + time expression
questions.explanation: Dramatic sentence: 'A new chapter begins'. 'from today' = starting point.
questions.grammarRule: A + new + chapter + begins + [from today / in my life]

questions.hindi: वह कभी हार नहीं मानती।
questions.english: She never gives up.
alternatives: She never surrenders.
She never admits defeat.
questions.hint: She + never + phrasal verb (no don't needed)
questions.explanation: 'gives up' = phrasal verb = हार मानना. She → gives (3rd person). 'never' already negative.

questions.hindi: मैं English में सोचना सीख रहा हूँ।
questions.english: I am learning to think in English.
alternatives: I am practicing thinking in English.
questions.hint: I + am + verb+ing + to + base verb + in + language
questions.explanation: 'am learning to think' = Present Continuous + infinitive. 'in English' = medium/language.
questions.grammarRule: I + am + learning + to + think + in + English

questions.hindi: वे लोग एक shared vision के साथ काम करते हैं।
questions.english: They work with a shared vision.
alternatives: They work towards a common vision.
questions.hint: They + base verb + with + article + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: They → work. 'with a shared vision' = preposition + article + adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: They + work + with + a + shared/common + vision

questions.hindi: मुझे लगता है कि English बहुत ज़रूरी है।
questions.english: I think English is very important.
alternatives: I believe English is very important.
In my opinion, English is very important.
questions.hint: I + think/believe + that + subject + be verb + adjective
questions.explanation: Opinion: I think + that + clause. 'that' optional here.

questions.hindi: वह बहुत कम लेकिन बहुत सोच-समझ के बोलती है।
questions.english: She speaks little but thoughtfully.
alternatives: She says less but with great thought.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + quantity adverb + conjunction + manner adverb
questions.explanation: She → speaks. 'little but thoughtfully' = contrast: little (quantity) but thoughtfully (manner).

questions.hindi: मुझे office में बहुत नए लोग मिले।
questions.english: I met many new people at the office.
alternatives: I encountered many new people at work.
questions.hint: I + past verb + many + adjective + plural noun + at + article + noun
questions.explanation: Meet → met (irregular past). 'many new people' = quantifier + adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: I + met + many + new + people + at + the + office

questions.hindi: वह सबसे ज़्यादा English में confident है।
questions.english: He is most confident in English.
alternatives: He is the most confident in English.
questions.hint: He + is + most/the most + adjective + in + language
questions.explanation: Superlative: most confident. 'in English' = area of confidence.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is + most + confident/comfortable + in + English

questions.hindi: मैं अपनी speaking skills को लेकर बहुत serious हूँ।
questions.english: I am very serious about improving my speaking skills.
alternatives: I take my speaking skills very seriously.
questions.hint: I + am + adverb + adjective + about + verb+ing + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'serious about improving' = adjective + preposition + gerund. 'my speaking skills' = possessive.
questions.grammarRule: I + am + very + serious + about + improving + my + [skills]

questions.hindi: वह एक role model की तरह है।
questions.english: She is like a role model.
alternatives: She is a role model.
She serves as a role model.
questions.hint: She + is + like + article + noun (compound)
questions.explanation: 'like a role model' = comparison preposition + article + compound noun.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is + like + a + role model/mentor/leader

questions.hindi: यह course मेरी life change करेगा।
questions.english: This course will change my life.
alternatives: This program will transform my life.
questions.hint: This + noun + will + base verb + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: Future: will + change. 'This course' = demonstrative + noun. 'my life' = possessive.

questions.hindi: हमारे CEO बहुत visionary हैं।
questions.english: Our CEO is very visionary.
alternatives: Our CEO has a very visionary mindset.
questions.hint: Our + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: 'Our CEO' = possessive + title (singular) → is. 'very visionary' = intensifier + adjective.

questions.hindi: मुझे अपने आप पर proud feel होता है।
questions.english: I feel proud of myself.
alternatives: I am proud of myself.
questions.hint: I + feel + adjective + of + reflexive pronoun
questions.explanation: 'proud of' = adjective + preposition. 'myself' = reflexive pronoun.

questions.hindi: वह बहुत जल्दी complex problems solve करता है।
questions.english: He solves complex problems very quickly.
alternatives: He resolves complex issues very fast.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + adjective + plural noun + adverb phrase
questions.explanation: He → solves. 'complex problems' = adjective + noun. 'very quickly' = manner adverb.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + solves + complex + problems + very + quickly

questions.hindi: वे लोग हर opportunity को seriously लेते हैं।
questions.english: They take every opportunity seriously.
alternatives: They treat every opportunity with seriousness.
questions.hint: They + base verb + every + noun + adverb
questions.explanation: They → take. 'every opportunity' = every + singular noun. 'seriously' = manner adverb.

questions.hindi: मैंने अभी-अभी यह course join किया।
questions.english: I just joined this course.
alternatives: I have just joined this course.
questions.hint: I + just + past verb + this + noun
questions.explanation: 'just' = very recently (simple past or present perfect both OK). Join → joined.

questions.hindi: वे दोनों एक-दूसरे को English में greet करते हैं।
questions.english: They both greet each other in English.
alternatives: Both of them greet each other in English.
questions.hint: They both + base verb + each other + in + language
questions.explanation: 'They both' = subject + intensifier. 'each other' = reciprocal. 'in English' = language.
questions.grammarRule: They + both + [greet/speak to] + each other + in + English

questions.hindi: वह किसी भी situation में comfortable रहता है।
questions.english: He is comfortable in any situation.
alternatives: He remains comfortable in any situation.
questions.hint: He + is + adjective + in + any + noun
questions.explanation: He → is. 'comfortable in' = adjective + preposition. 'any situation' = any + noun.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is + comfortable + in + any + situation

questions.hindi: मुझे आज बहुत busy schedule है।
questions.english: I have a very busy schedule today.
alternatives: My schedule is very busy today.
questions.hint: I + have + article + adverb + adjective + noun + time
questions.explanation: I → have. 'a very busy schedule' = article + intensifier + adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: I + have + a + very + busy + schedule + today

questions.hindi: वह English में errors ढूँढ कर correct करती है।
questions.english: She finds and corrects errors in English.
alternatives: She identifies and fixes errors in English.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + and + verb+s + plural noun + in + language
questions.explanation: She → finds, corrects (compound predicate, both 3rd person). 'in English' = context.
questions.grammarRule: She + finds + and + corrects + [errors] + in + English

questions.hindi: मेरे सामने बहुत opportunities हैं।
questions.english: There are many opportunities in front of me.
alternatives: I have many opportunities ahead of me.
questions.hint: There + are + many + plural noun + in front of + object pronoun
questions.explanation: 'There are many' = plural existence. 'in front of me' = prepositional phrase.
questions.grammarRule: There + are + many + opportunities + in front of + me

questions.hindi: वह अपने doubts को immediately clear करता है।
questions.english: He clears his doubts immediately.
alternatives: He resolves his doubts right away.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + possessive + plural noun + adverb
questions.explanation: He → clears (3rd person). 'his doubts' = possessive. 'immediately' = time adverb.

questions.hindi: मेरी यह journey Day 1 से शुरू होती है।
questions.english: This journey of mine begins from Day 1.
alternatives: My journey begins from Day 1.
questions.hint: This + noun + of + possessive pronoun + verb+s + from + proper noun
questions.explanation: 'this journey of mine' = formal possessive structure. 'begins from' = starting point.
questions.grammarRule: This + [journey/chapter] + of + mine + begins + from + Day + [number]

questions.hindi: वे हमेशा एक-दूसरे को encourage करते हैं।
questions.english: They always encourage each other.
alternatives: They constantly motivate each other.
questions.hint: They + always + base verb + each other
questions.explanation: They → encourage (base verb). 'always' = frequency adverb.

questions.hindi: वह meeting के बाद follow-up email भेजती है।
questions.english: She sends a follow-up email after the meeting.
alternatives: She follows up with an email after every meeting.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + article + noun + noun + after + article + noun
questions.explanation: She → sends (3rd person). 'a follow-up email' = article + compound adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: She + sends + a + follow-up + email + after + the + meeting

questions.hindi: मैं हर feedback को positively लेता हूँ।
questions.english: I take every feedback positively.
alternatives: I receive every feedback in a positive way.
questions.hint: I + base verb + every + noun + adverb
questions.explanation: I → take. 'every feedback' = every + uncountable noun (used as singular). 'positively' = adverb.

questions.hindi: वे लोग नई technologies बहुत जल्दी adopt कर लेते हैं।
questions.english: They adopt new technologies very quickly.
alternatives: They embrace new technologies very fast.
questions.hint: They + base verb + adjective + plural noun + adverb phrase
questions.explanation: They → adopt. 'new technologies' = adjective + plural noun. 'very quickly' = adverb phrase.
questions.grammarRule: They + adopt + new + technologies + very + quickly

questions.hindi: मुझे यह step-by-step समझना होगा।
questions.english: I will need to understand this step by step.
alternatives: I need to understand this step by step.
questions.hint: I + will need to + base verb + this + adverb phrase
questions.explanation: 'step by step' = manner adverb phrase = क्रम से. 'will need to' = future obligation.
questions.grammarRule: I + need to + understand + this + step by step

questions.hindi: वह HR interview में बहुत professional था।
questions.english: He was very professional in the HR interview.
alternatives: He came across as very professional in the HR interview.
questions.hint: He + was + adverb + adjective + in + the + noun + noun
questions.explanation: Past: was. 'very professional' = intensifier + adjective. 'in the HR interview' = location/context.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + was + very + professional + in + the + [interview/meeting]

questions.hindi: मैं English speaking में कम confident था लेकिन अब confident हूँ।
questions.english: I was less confident in English speaking, but now I am confident.
alternatives: I used to be less confident in English, but now I am more confident.
questions.hint: I + was + comparative + adjective + in + noun + but + now + I + am + adjective
questions.explanation: Contrast: past (was less confident) vs. present (am confident). 'but now' = contrast.
questions.grammarRule: I + was + less + confident + before + but + now + I + am + [more] + confident

questions.hindi: यह पूरा course मेरे लिए एक gift है।
questions.english: This entire course is a gift for me.
alternatives: This whole course is a blessing for me.
questions.hint: This + adjective + noun + is + article + noun + for + object pronoun
questions.explanation: 'This entire course' = demonstrative + adjective + noun. 'a gift' = article + noun. 'for me' = beneficiary.
questions.grammarRule: This + [entire/whole] + course + is + a + gift/blessing + for + me

questions.hindi: कृपया यह form भर दें।
questions.english: Please fill in this form.
alternatives: Please complete this form.
Please fill out this form.
questions.hint: Please + base verb + this + noun
questions.explanation: Polite imperative: Please + base verb. 'fill in/out' = phrasal verbs for form completion.

questions.hindi: कृपया मेरी seat बचा कर रखें।
questions.english: Please save my seat.
alternatives: Please keep my seat reserved.
questions.hint: Please + base verb + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: Polite request: Please + save (base verb) + my seat (possessive + noun).

questions.hindi: सर, क्या मैं 5 मिनट के लिए बाहर जा सकता हूँ?
questions.english: Sir, may I go out for 5 minutes?
alternatives: Sir, can I step out for 5 minutes?
questions.hint: Sir, may/can + I + base verb + out + for + duration?
questions.explanation: Permission request: May I / Can I + base verb. 'for 5 minutes' = duration.

questions.hindi: क्या मैं इस meeting में attend कर सकता हूँ?
questions.english: May I attend this meeting?
alternatives: Can I join this meeting?
Am I allowed to attend this meeting?
questions.hint: May + I + base verb + this + noun?
questions.explanation: Permission: May I + base verb. 'this meeting' = demonstrative + noun.

questions.hindi: मुझे एक और chance दीजिए।
questions.english: Please give me one more chance.
alternatives: Please allow me one more chance.
questions.hint: Please + base verb + me + number + more + noun
questions.explanation: Polite request. 'give me' = indirect object. 'one more chance' = one + more + noun.
questions.grammarRule: Please + give + me + one more + chance/opportunity

questions.hindi: वह interview के लिए तैयार हो रही है।
questions.english: She is preparing for the interview.
alternatives: She is getting ready for the interview.
questions.hint: She + is + verb+ing + for + the + noun
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: is + preparing. 'for the interview' = purpose preposition.
questions.grammarRule: She + is + preparing + for + the + interview

questions.hindi: मैंने अपनी पूरी team को thank किया।
questions.english: I thanked my entire team.
alternatives: I expressed gratitude to my whole team.
questions.hint: I + past verb + possessive + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Thank → thanked (regular past). 'my entire team' = possessive + adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: वह एक पल के लिए रुका और फिर बोला।
questions.english: He paused for a moment and then spoke.
alternatives: He stopped for a moment and then said something.
questions.hint: He + past verb + for + article + noun + and + then + past verb
questions.explanation: Sequence: paused (V2) + and then + spoke (V2 irregular). 'for a moment' = duration.
questions.grammarRule: He + [paused/stopped] + for a moment + and then + [spoke/continued]

questions.hindi: मेरे पास एक सवाल है।
questions.english: I have a question.
alternatives: I'd like to ask a question.
questions.hint: I + have + article + noun
questions.explanation: Simple: I + have + a + question. Very common expression.

questions.hindi: क्या मुझे कोई और document देना होगा?
questions.english: Do I need to provide any other document?
alternatives: Will I need to submit any more documents?
Is any additional document required?
questions.hint: Do + I + need to + base verb + any + adjective + noun?
questions.explanation: 'Do I need to' = obligation question. 'any other document' = any + other + singular noun.
questions.grammarRule: Do + I + need to + provide/submit + any + other + document?

questions.hindi: हमें अपनी daily English practice जारी रखनी है।
questions.english: We need to continue our daily English practice.
alternatives: We should keep up our daily English practice.
questions.hint: We + need to + base verb + possessive + adjective + noun + noun
questions.explanation: 'continue our daily English practice' = verb + possessive + adjective + compound noun.
questions.grammarRule: We + need to + continue + our + daily + [English/language] + practice

questions.hindi: वह meeting में सबसे पहले बोलती है।
questions.english: She is the first to speak in the meeting.
alternatives: She speaks first in every meeting.
questions.hint: She + is + the + first + to + base verb + in + article + noun
questions.explanation: 'the first to speak' = superlative structure + infinitive. 'in the meeting' = context.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is + the + first + to + speak + in + the + meeting

questions.hindi: मैंने आज बहुत कुछ सीखा।
questions.english: I learned a lot today.
alternatives: I learnt so much today.
I gained a lot of knowledge today.
questions.hint: I + past verb + a lot + time adverb
questions.explanation: Learn → learned/learnt (regular past). 'a lot' = quantity. 'today' = time.
questions.grammarRule: I + learned + a lot + today/this week

questions.hindi: वह positive feedback से motivated होती है।
questions.english: She gets motivated by positive feedback.
alternatives: Positive feedback motivates her.
She is motivated by positive feedback.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + adjective + by + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'gets motivated by' = passive-like expression. 'positive feedback' = adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: She + gets + motivated + by + positive + feedback

questions.hindi: मैं अपने जीवन में English को सबसे ज़रूरी मानता हूँ।
questions.english: I consider English the most important thing in my life.
alternatives: I think English is the most important skill in my life.
questions.hint: I + base verb + noun + the + superlative + noun + in + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'consider + object + adjective' = SVOC pattern. 'the most important thing' = superlative.
questions.grammarRule: I + consider + English + the most important + [thing/skill] + in my life

questions.hindi: वे लोग English को एक tool की तरह use करते हैं।
questions.english: They use English as a tool.
alternatives: They treat English as a tool.
They employ English as a tool.
questions.hint: They + base verb + noun + as + article + noun
questions.explanation: 'use as' = verb + preposition pattern. 'as a tool' = comparison/role.
questions.grammarRule: They + use + English + as + a + tool/medium

questions.hindi: वह अपनी weaknesses को strengths में बदल देती है।
questions.english: She turns her weaknesses into strengths.
alternatives: She converts her weaknesses into strengths.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + possessive + plural noun + into + plural noun
questions.explanation: She → turns (3rd person). 'into' = transformation preposition. 'weaknesses...strengths' = contrast.

questions.hindi: मुझे रोज़ नए लोगों से मिलना पसंद है।
questions.english: I like meeting new people every day.
alternatives: I enjoy meeting new people daily.
questions.hint: I + like/enjoy + verb+ing + adjective + plural noun + every day
questions.explanation: like + gerund (meeting). 'new people' = adjective + plural noun. 'every day' = frequency.
questions.grammarRule: I + like + meeting + new + people + every day

questions.hindi: वह जो कहता है वो करता है।
questions.english: He does what he says.
alternatives: He practices what he preaches.
He walks the talk.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + what + he + verb+s (relative clause)
questions.explanation: Relative clause: 'what he says' = object clause. 'does what he says' = integrity.

questions.hindi: मैं future में एक great speaker बनूँगा।
questions.english: I will become a great speaker in the future.
alternatives: I will be a great speaker one day.
questions.hint: I + will + become + article + adjective + noun + in + the + noun
questions.explanation: Future: will + become. 'a great speaker' = article + adjective + noun. 'in the future' = time.
questions.grammarRule: I + will + become + a + great + speaker + in the future

questions.hindi: वे सब एक-दूसरे की respect करते हैं।
questions.english: They all respect each other.
alternatives: All of them respect one another.
questions.hint: They all + base verb + each other
questions.explanation: 'they all' = emphasis. 'each other/one another' = reciprocal pronouns.

questions.hindi: मेरे दादाजी English बोल सकते थे।
questions.english: My grandfather could speak English.
alternatives: My grandfather was able to speak English.
questions.hint: My + noun + could + base verb + language
questions.explanation: Past ability: could + base verb. 'My grandfather' = possessive + family noun.

questions.hindi: वह office में English में ही बात करती है।
questions.english: She talks only in English at the office.
alternatives: She only speaks English at work.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + only + in + language + at + article + noun
questions.explanation: She → talks. 'only in English' = restrictive adverb. 'at the office' = location.
questions.grammarRule: She + talks/speaks + only + in + English + at + the + office

questions.hindi: मुझे confidence होता जा रहा है।
questions.english: I am becoming more confident.
alternatives: I am gaining more confidence.
My confidence is growing.
questions.hint: I + am + verb+ing + comparative + adjective
questions.explanation: 'becoming more confident' = present continuous + comparative. 'more confident' = comparative.

questions.hindi: वह हर situation में solution निकाल लेता है।
questions.english: He finds a solution in every situation.
alternatives: He comes up with a solution in every situation.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + article + noun + in + every + noun
questions.explanation: He → finds (3rd person). 'in every situation' = in + every + singular noun.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + finds + a + solution + in + every + situation

questions.hindi: मैं अपने students को बहुत care के साथ पढ़ाता हूँ।
questions.english: I teach my students with great care.
alternatives: I teach my students very carefully.
questions.hint: I + base verb + possessive + plural noun + with + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: I → teach (base verb). 'with great care' = prepositional manner phrase.
questions.grammarRule: I + teach + my + students + with + great + care

questions.hindi: वह India की culture के बारे में बहुत passionate है।
questions.english: She is very passionate about Indian culture.
alternatives: She is deeply passionate about India's culture.
questions.hint: She + is + adverb + adjective + about + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'passionate about' = adjective + preposition. 'Indian culture' = adjective + noun.

questions.hindi: मैं अपने family के लिए कुछ बड़ा करना चाहता हूँ।
questions.english: I want to do something great for my family.
alternatives: I want to achieve something great for my family.
questions.hint: I + want to + base verb + something + adjective + for + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'want to do' = want + infinitive. 'something great' = indefinite pronoun + adjective (after pronoun). 'for my family' = beneficiary.
questions.grammarRule: I + want to + do + something + great/big + for + my + family

questions.hindi: वे लोग meeting में हमेशा agenda follow करते हैं।
questions.english: They always follow the agenda in meetings.
alternatives: They stick to the agenda in every meeting.
questions.hint: They + always + base verb + the + noun + in + plural noun
questions.explanation: They → follow. 'the agenda' = specific (definite article). 'in meetings' = context.
questions.grammarRule: They + always + follow + the + agenda + in + meetings

questions.hindi: मुझे पता है कि मैं एक दिन English में expert बनूँगा।
questions.english: I know that I will become an expert in English one day.
alternatives: I am confident that I will master English one day.
questions.hint: I + know + that + I + will + become + article + noun + in + language + time
questions.explanation: Reported: I know + that + clause. 'an expert' = article (vowel) + noun. 'one day' = future time.
questions.grammarRule: I + know + that + I + will + become + an + expert + in + English

questions.hindi: वह रोज़ आईने के सामने English बोलती है।
questions.english: She speaks English in front of the mirror every day.
alternatives: She practices English in front of the mirror daily.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + language + in front of + the + noun + every day
questions.explanation: She → speaks. 'in front of the mirror' = position preposition phrase. 'every day' = frequency.
questions.grammarRule: She + practices/speaks + English + in front of + the mirror + every day

questions.hindi: मैं हर रात सोने से पहले English podcast सुनता हूँ।
questions.english: I listen to an English podcast every night before sleeping.
alternatives: I listen to English podcasts before bed every night.
questions.hint: I + listen to + article + adjective + noun + every night + before + verb+ing
questions.explanation: 'listen to' = verb + preposition. 'before sleeping' = before + gerund.
questions.grammarRule: I + listen to + an English + podcast + every night + before + sleeping

questions.hindi: वह interview में बिल्कुल सही answers देता है।
questions.english: He gives exactly the right answers in the interview.
alternatives: He answers perfectly in the interview.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + adverb + the + adjective + plural noun + in + article + noun
questions.explanation: He → gives. 'exactly the right answers' = adverb + adjective + plural noun.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + gives + exactly + the + right + answers + in + the + interview

questions.hindi: हम सब मिलकर एक बड़ा impact create करेंगे।
questions.english: Together, we will create a big impact.
alternatives: We will all make a big difference together.
questions.hint: Together, + we + will + base verb + article + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: 'Together' = adverb of manner (placed first). Future: will + create.
questions.grammarRule: Together, + we + will + create + a + [big/positive] + impact

questions.hindi: वह अपनी company का brand ambassador है।
questions.english: He is the brand ambassador of his company.
alternatives: She is his company's brand ambassador.
questions.hint: He + is + the + compound noun + of + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'the brand ambassador' = specific title. 'of his company' = possession.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is + the + [brand ambassador/representative] + of + his/her + company

questions.hindi: मेरे teacher ने मुझे encourage किया।
questions.english: My teacher encouraged me.
alternatives: My teacher motivated me.
questions.hint: My + noun + past verb + me
questions.explanation: Encourage → encouraged (regular past). 'me' = object pronoun.

questions.hindi: वे लोग एक sustainable business model बनाना चाहते हैं।
questions.english: They want to build a sustainable business model.
alternatives: They wish to develop a sustainable business model.
questions.hint: They + want to + base verb + article + adjective + compound noun
questions.explanation: They → want (base verb). 'a sustainable business model' = article + adjective + compound noun.
questions.grammarRule: They + want to + build + a + sustainable + [model/system]

questions.hindi: मैं अपने experience से लोगों को सिखाना चाहता हूँ।
questions.english: I want to teach people from my experience.
alternatives: I want to share my experience with others.
questions.hint: I + want to + base verb + people + from + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: 'want to teach' = want + infinitive. 'from my experience' = source preposition.
questions.grammarRule: I + want to + teach/help + people + from + my + experience

questions.hindi: उसकी आवाज़ बहुत clear है।
questions.english: Her voice is very clear.
alternatives: His voice is very clear.
Her voice is very articulate.
questions.hint: His/Her + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: 'Her voice' = possessive + noun. 'is very clear' = be verb + intensifier + adjective.

questions.hindi: मेरा सपना है कि मैं एक दिन TED Talk दूँगा।
questions.english: My dream is to give a TED Talk one day.
alternatives: I dream of giving a TED Talk one day.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + to + base verb + article + proper noun + time
questions.explanation: 'My dream is to' = possessive + noun + be verb + infinitive. 'one day' = future.
questions.grammarRule: My + dream + is + to + [give/deliver] + a + TED Talk + one day

questions.hindi: वह English में इस तरह बोलती है जैसे native speaker हो।
questions.english: She speaks English like a native speaker.
alternatives: She sounds like a native English speaker.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + language + like + article + compound noun
questions.explanation: She → speaks. 'like a native speaker' = comparison preposition + article + noun.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + speaks + English + like + a + native + speaker

questions.hindi: मुझे हर English word pronounce करना पसंद है।
questions.english: I like to pronounce every English word correctly.
alternatives: I enjoy pronouncing every English word.
questions.hint: I + like to + base verb + every + adjective + noun + adverb
questions.explanation: 'like to pronounce' = like + infinitive. 'every English word' = every + adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: I + like to + pronounce + every + English + word + correctly

questions.hindi: वे लोग office में positive environment maintain करते हैं।
questions.english: They maintain a positive environment in the office.
alternatives: They create a positive work environment.
questions.hint: They + base verb + article + adjective + noun + in + article + noun
questions.explanation: They → maintain. 'a positive environment' = article + adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: They + maintain + a + positive + environment + at the office

questions.hindi: मुझे English सीखने में बहुत मज़ा आता है।
questions.english: I enjoy learning English very much.
alternatives: I have a lot of fun learning English.
I really enjoy learning English.
questions.hint: I + enjoy + verb+ing + language + adverb phrase
questions.explanation: enjoy + gerund. 'very much' = degree adverb after object.
questions.grammarRule: I + enjoy/love + learning + English + very much

questions.hindi: वह crisis में भी calm रहता है।
questions.english: He stays calm even in a crisis.
alternatives: He remains calm even during a crisis.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + adjective + even + in/during + article + noun
questions.explanation: 'even in a crisis' = emphasis with 'even'. He → stays (linking verb).
questions.grammarRule: He/She + stays + calm + even + in + a + crisis

questions.hindi: मैं एक day English में सोचूँगा।
questions.english: One day, I will think in English.
alternatives: I will think in English someday.
questions.hint: One day + I + will + base verb + in + language
questions.explanation: Future: will + think. 'One day' = future unspecified time (placed at start for emphasis).
questions.grammarRule: One day, + I + will + think/dream + in + English

questions.hindi: वे सब किसी न किसी topic में expert हैं।
questions.english: They are all experts in some topic or the other.
alternatives: Each of them is an expert in at least one topic.
questions.hint: They + are + all + plural noun + in + some + noun + or + the other
questions.explanation: 'some or the other' = indefinite expression. 'experts in' = plural noun + preposition.
questions.grammarRule: They + are + all + experts + in + some + [field/topic]

questions.hindi: वह कभी नहीं कहता 'मैं नहीं जानता' — वह हमेशा solution ढूँढता है।
questions.english: He never says 'I don't know' — he always finds a solution.
alternatives: He never admits not knowing — he always finds a way.
questions.hint: He + never + verb+s + quote + dash + he + always + verb+s + article + noun
questions.explanation: Compound sentence with contrast. 'never' vs 'always'. Proactive character.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + never + says + 'I don't know' — he/she + always + finds + a + solution

questions.hindi: मुझे अपना goal याद दिलाओ जब मैं हार मानने लगूँ।
questions.english: Remind me of my goal when I am about to give up.
alternatives: Tell me my goal whenever I feel like giving up.
questions.hint: Imperative: base verb + me + of + possessive + noun + when + subordinate clause
questions.explanation: Imperative: Remind (base verb). 'about to give up' = about to + phrasal verb.
questions.grammarRule: Remind + me + of + my + goal + when + I + [feel like giving up]

questions.hindi: वह रोज़ 30 English sentences लिखती है।
questions.english: She writes 30 English sentences every day.
alternatives: She writes 30 sentences in English daily.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + number + adjective + plural noun + every day
questions.explanation: She → writes. '30 English sentences' = number + adjective + noun. 'every day' = frequency.
questions.grammarRule: She + writes + [number] + English + sentences + every day

questions.hindi: मुझे पता है कि यह journey आसान नहीं है।
questions.english: I know this journey is not easy.
alternatives: I understand this journey is not easy.
questions.hint: I + know + that + this + noun + is + not + adjective
questions.explanation: I know + that + clause. 'this journey' = demonstrative + noun. 'not easy' = negative adjective.
questions.grammarRule: I + know + (that) + this + journey + is + not + easy

questions.hindi: लेकिन मैं हार नहीं मानूँगा।
questions.english: But I will not give up.
alternatives: But I won't give up.
But I won't quit.
questions.hint: But + I + will + not + phrasal verb
questions.explanation: 'will not/won't' = future negative. 'give up' = phrasal verb = हार मानना.
questions.grammarRule: But + I + will + not + give up

questions.hindi: वह जो कुछ भी करती है उसमें दिल लगाती है।
questions.english: She puts her heart into whatever she does.
alternatives: She gives her all in everything she does.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + possessive + noun + into + whatever + she + verb+s
questions.explanation: 'puts her heart into' = expression. 'whatever she does' = indefinite relative clause.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + puts + her/his + heart + into + whatever + she/he + does

questions.hindi: मुझे उन सभी लोगों से मिलना है जो English में fluent हैं।
questions.english: I want to meet all the people who are fluent in English.
alternatives: I wish to meet everyone who speaks English fluently.
questions.hint: I + want to + base verb + all + the + people + who + are + adjective + in + language
questions.explanation: 'who are fluent in English' = relative clause. 'all the people' = quantifier + specific.
questions.grammarRule: I + want to + meet + people + who + are + fluent + in + English

questions.hindi: वह office में हमेशा एक positive change लाता है।
questions.english: He always brings a positive change to the office.
alternatives: He always makes a positive impact at the office.
questions.hint: He + always + verb+s + article + adjective + noun + to + article + noun
questions.explanation: He → brings (3rd person). 'a positive change' = article + adjective + noun. 'to the office' = direction.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + always + brings + a + positive + change + to the office

questions.hindi: मैं जितना more practice करूँगा उतना better बनूँगा।
questions.english: The more I practice, the better I will become.
alternatives: More practice will make me better.
questions.hint: The + comparative + subject + verb, + the + comparative + subject + will + base verb
questions.explanation: 'The more...the better' = double comparative structure.
questions.grammarRule: The + more + I + practice, + the + better + I + will + become

questions.hindi: वह बात करने से पहले सोचती है।
questions.english: She thinks before she speaks.
alternatives: She thinks before speaking.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + before + she + verb+s (subordinate clause)
questions.explanation: 'thinks before she speaks' = main clause + before + subordinate clause. Both present.

questions.hindi: मैं आज से हर रोज़ English practice करूँगा।
questions.english: I will practice English every day from today.
alternatives: Starting today, I will practice English every day.
questions.hint: I + will + base verb + language + every day + from + time adverb
questions.explanation: Future commitment: will + practice. 'from today' = starting point.
questions.grammarRule: I + will + practice + English + every day + from + today

questions.hindi: वे लोग एक-दूसरे की success से खुश होते हैं।
questions.english: They are happy at each other's success.
alternatives: They rejoice in each other's success.
questions.hint: They + are + adjective + at + each other's + noun
questions.explanation: 'at each other's success' = preposition + reciprocal possessive + noun.
questions.grammarRule: They + are + happy + at/for + each other's + success

questions.hindi: मैं इस course को complete करने का वादा करता हूँ।
questions.english: I promise to complete this course.
alternatives: I commit to completing this course.
I am determined to complete this course.
questions.hint: I + verb + to + base verb + this + noun
questions.explanation: 'promise to + infinitive' = verb + infinitive pattern. 'this course' = demonstrative + noun.

questions.hindi: वह बहुत कम बोलती है लेकिन जब बोलती है तो meaningful बोलती है।
questions.english: She speaks little, but when she speaks, it is meaningful.
alternatives: She says little but what she says is meaningful.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + little, + but + when + she + verb+s, + it + is + adjective
questions.explanation: Compound-complex: two contrasting clauses with 'when' subordinate.
questions.grammarRule: She + speaks + little + but + when + she + speaks + it + is + meaningful

questions.hindi: मैं 75 दिन बाद एक different person बनूँगा।
questions.english: I will be a different person after 75 days.
alternatives: After 75 days, I will become a different person.
questions.hint: I + will + be/become + article + adjective + noun + after + duration
questions.explanation: Future: will + be. 'after 75 days' = time expression.
questions.grammarRule: I + will + be + a + different/better + person + after + [duration]

questions.hindi: वह speaking में जितना confident है grammar में उतना नहीं।
questions.english: He is more confident in speaking than in grammar.
alternatives: He is better at speaking than at grammar.
questions.hint: He + is + comparative + adjective + in + noun + than + in + noun
questions.explanation: Comparative: more confident than. 'in speaking...in grammar' = two contrasted areas.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is + more + [confident/skilled] + in + [area1] + than + in + [area2]

questions.hindi: मेरा goal सिर्फ English सीखना नहीं है — मेरा goal है English में सोचना।
questions.english: My goal is not just to learn English — my goal is to think in English.
alternatives: I don't just want to learn English — I want to think in English.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + not + just + to + base verb + language + dash + my + noun + is + to + base verb + in + language
questions.explanation: Compound emphatic sentence with dash. 'not just...but' contrast.
questions.grammarRule: My + goal + is + not just + to + learn + English — it is + to + think + in + English

questions.hindi: वे लोग जो practice नहीं करते वो English fluent नहीं बनते।
questions.english: Those who do not practice do not become fluent.
alternatives: People who don't practice never become fluent.
questions.hint: Those + who + do not + base verb + do not + become + adjective
questions.explanation: 'Those who' = relative pronoun for people. Subject of main clause.
questions.grammarRule: Those + who + do not + practice + do not + become + fluent

questions.hindi: मैंने decide किया है कि मैं रोज़ practice करूँगा।
questions.english: I have decided to practice every day.
alternatives: I have made up my mind to practice every day.
questions.hint: I + have + past V3 + to + base verb + every day
questions.explanation: Present Perfect: have + decided (V3). 'to practice' = infinitive.
questions.grammarRule: I + have + decided + to + practice + every day

questions.hindi: वह हर meeting में अपनी राय clearly रखती है।
questions.english: She clearly states her opinion in every meeting.
alternatives: She expresses her views clearly in every meeting.
questions.hint: She + adverb + verb+s + possessive + noun + in + every + noun
questions.explanation: 'clearly states' = adverb + verb. She → states. 'in every meeting' = context.
questions.grammarRule: She + clearly + states/expresses + her + opinion + in + every + meeting

questions.hindi: मुझे English में बात करने में ab confidence आ गया है।
questions.english: I have gained confidence in speaking English now.
alternatives: I now feel confident speaking English.
questions.hint: I + have + past V3 + noun + in + verb+ing + language + now
questions.explanation: Present Perfect: have + gained (V3). 'in speaking English' = gerund phrase. 'now' = present relevance.
questions.grammarRule: I + have + gained + confidence + in + speaking + English

questions.hindi: वे लोग हर challenge को एक lesson की तरह देखते हैं।
questions.english: They see every challenge as a lesson.
alternatives: They view every challenge as a learning experience.
questions.hint: They + base verb + every + noun + as + article + noun
questions.explanation: They → see. 'as a lesson' = role/comparison. 'every challenge' = every + singular noun.
questions.grammarRule: They + see + every + challenge + as + a + lesson/opportunity

questions.hindi: मैं English को इस तरह सीखूँगा कि लोग देखते रह जाएंगे।
questions.english: I will learn English in such a way that people will be amazed.
alternatives: I will learn English so well that everyone will be surprised.
questions.hint: I + will + base verb + language + in such a way + that + subject + will + be + adjective
questions.explanation: Result clause: 'in such a way that...'. Future: will learn. Complex motivation.
questions.grammarRule: I + will + learn + English + in such a way + that + people + will + be + amazed

questions.hindi: वह जो शुरू करता है उसे पूरा करता है।
questions.english: He finishes what he starts.
alternatives: He completes everything he starts.
questions.hint: He + verb+s + what + he + verb+s (relative clause)
questions.explanation: 'what he starts' = object relative clause. 'finishes what' = verb + relative.

questions.hindi: मैं अपने आप को हर दिन एक बेहतर version बनाता हूँ।
questions.english: I make myself a better version every day.
alternatives: I become a better version of myself every day.
questions.hint: I + base verb + reflexive + article + comparative + adjective + noun + every day
questions.explanation: 'make myself better' = SVOC with reflexive. 'a better version' = article + comparative + noun.
questions.grammarRule: I + make + myself + a + better + version + every day

questions.hindi: उनसे मिलकर मुझे बहुत कुछ सीखने को मिला।
questions.english: Meeting them taught me a lot.
alternatives: I learned a lot by meeting them.
questions.hint: Verb+ing + object + past verb + me + a lot
questions.explanation: Gerund as subject: Meeting them = subject. 'taught me' = verb + indirect object. 'a lot' = quantity.

questions.hindi: वह success के बाद भी humble रहती है।
questions.english: She remains humble even after success.
alternatives: She stays humble even after achieving success.
questions.hint: She + verb+s + adjective + even + after + noun
questions.explanation: 'even after success' = emphasis. She → remains (linking verb). 'humble' = adjective.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + remains + humble + even + after + success

questions.hindi: मुझे पक्का यकीन है कि मैं success करूँगा।
questions.english: I am absolutely sure that I will succeed.
alternatives: I am completely confident that I will be successful.
questions.hint: I + am + adverb + adjective + that + I + will + base verb
questions.explanation: 'absolutely sure' = adverb + adjective. 'that I will succeed' = noun clause.
questions.grammarRule: I + am + absolutely + sure + that + I + will + succeed

questions.hindi: हम एक ऐसी दुनिया में जी रहे हैं जहाँ English ज़रूरी है।
questions.english: We are living in a world where English is essential.
alternatives: We live in a world where English is necessary.
questions.hint: We + are + verb+ing + in + article + noun + where + relative clause
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: are + living. 'where English is essential' = relative clause.
questions.grammarRule: We + are + living + in + a + world + where + English + is + essential

questions.hindi: वह एक dream देखता है और उसे पूरा करने के लिए काम करता है।
questions.english: He has a dream and works hard to achieve it.
alternatives: He dreams and works relentlessly to achieve it.
questions.hint: He + has + article + noun + and + verb+s + adverb + to + base verb + object
questions.explanation: Compound predicate: has a dream + and + works hard. 'to achieve it' = infinitive of purpose.
questions.grammarRule: He/She + has + a + dream + and + works + hard + to + achieve + it

questions.hindi: मैं 75 दिन पूरे करने के बाद रुकूँगा नहीं।
questions.english: I will not stop after completing 75 days.
alternatives: I won't stop even after finishing 75 days.
questions.hint: I + will + not + base verb + after + verb+ing + number + plural noun
questions.explanation: Future negative: will not + stop. 'after completing' = after + gerund.
questions.grammarRule: I + will + not + stop + after + completing + [the course]

questions.hindi: यह English journey मेरी पूरी ज़िंदगी बदल देगी।
questions.english: This English journey will change my entire life.
alternatives: This journey of learning English will transform my whole life.
questions.hint: This + noun + noun + will + base verb + possessive + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Future: will + change. 'my entire life' = possessive + adjective + noun.
questions.grammarRule: This + English + journey + will + change + my + entire + life

questions.hindi: मैं हर रोज़ practice करूँगा, हर रोज़ सीखूँगा, हर रोज़ बेहतर बनूँगा।
questions.english: I will practice every day, learn every day, and get better every day.
alternatives: Every day, I will practice, learn, and improve.
questions.hint: I + will + base verb + every day, + base verb + every day, + and + base verb + adjective + every day
questions.explanation: Triple future commitment with anaphora. Parallel structure.
questions.grammarRule: I + will + practice / learn / get better + every single day
